Ag brath ar chúinsí an Údaráis, b’fhéidir nach cuid den Chlár Iompair ná den Eagraíocht do thuismitheoirí iad torthaí agus moltaí a scaipeadh agus monatóireacht a dhéanamh ar thionchar, cé go bhféadfadh freagrachtaí tábhachtacha a bheith orthu.<br/><br/>Tá creat eile ann do ÚPA a chuireann an Comhar Eorpach um Mheasúnú Teicneolaíochta Sláinte (Busse 2002) ar fáil, mar a leanas.<br/><br/>Iarratas ar mheasúnú a dhéanamh/ sainaithint riachtanais mheasúnaithe<br/><br/>Tosaíocht<br/><br/>Coimisiúnú<br/><br/>An measúnú a dhéanamh<br/><br/>Sainmhíniú ar cheist/cheisteanna beartais<br/><br/>An prótacal HTA a bhunú<br/><br/>Faisnéis chúlra/cinneadh maidir le stádas na teicneolaíochta a bhailiú<br/><br/>Sainmhíniú ar na ceisteanna taighde<br/><br/>Foinsí sonraí, breithmheas fianaise, agus sintéis fianaise do gach ceann de na:<br/><br/>Sábháilteacht<br/><br/>Éifeachtúlacht/éifeachtacht<br/><br/>Síceolaíocht, sóisialta, eiticiúil<br/><br/>Eagraíocht, gairmiúil<br/><br/>Eacnamaíoch<br/><br/>Dréacht-saotharlann díospóireachta, conclúidí agus moltaí<br/><br/>Athbhreithniú seachtrach<br/><br/>Tuarascáil deiridh an Údaráis agus an tuarascáil achoimre a fhoilsiú<br/><br/>Scaipeadh<br/><br/>Úsáid HTA<br/><br/>Nuashonrú ar an TAC<br/><br/>Mar a léirítear le caibidil éagsúla agus ceannteidil na rannóige, déantar cur síos ar gach ceann de na bunchéimeanna HAA atá liostaithe thuas sa doiciméad seo. |
| The heretical armies of the Kingdom continue to attack the Empire. The traitors come to destroy all order, while the Kingdoms Skyle and Redzamiba continue to plot the overthrow of Imperial authority. It's not a good day to be part of the Empire.<br/><br/>The Emperor rules with a slowly crumbling hand, untrusting and friendless, he directs his Legions in a futile attempt to stop the inevitable as enemies close in from every direction.<br/><br/>The Emperor is weak, and must be put down. You are the one to do it. A noble-born Lord in the Capital, it is time for you to begin the long and hard journey of removing the Emperor and assuming his Throne. Regicide.<br/><br/>Circles in the Capital<br/>There are several distinct bodies within the Empire and its Capital, each different yet similar, and each pliant and malleable to those seeking a greater destiny. They are:<br/><br/>The Senate, whose job is to listen to the people, and bring their plight to the Emperor.<br/>The War Council, whose job is to counsel the Emperor in the formation of battle plans. And the<br/>Legions, the military arm of the Emperor.<br/>The Auxiliaries, armies of volunteer peasants.<br/><br/>SGS<br/><br/>17th January 2012, 03:23<br/><br/>Count me in.<br/><br/>Oh, but where is Virgil and his crusader knights? You'll play a new character though.<br/><br/>SGS<br/><br/>17th January 2012, 03:39<br/><br/>As long as you don't expect me to fight the Kingdom. They're not just any heretics, they're MY heretics.<br/><br/>Silas<br/><br/>17th January 2012, 04:22<br/><br/>You have my wit. I'm in.<br/><br/>thegreatergood<br/><br/>17th January 2012, 12:34<br/><br/>I'll play<br/><br/>DrakonTheNightLord<br/><br/>18th January 2012, 07:12<br/><br/>We will begin our game! Choose from the following starting choices, and state your character name.<br/><br/>Pyrosikth<br/>1) You are an insidious spy of the Kingdom of Redzamiba, sent to depose the Emperor and establish a more tolerant regime in the name of King Nintendos Aurelios!<br/><br/>2) You are a lowly Captain of the First Legion. Tired at the death and destruction and the callousness of the Emperor's tactics, sending whole Legions into suicidal death missions and His total disregard for the peasants under his aegis has left you disillusioned. It is time for a new Emperor.<br/><br/>3) Cunning and ruthless, you have managed to claim a place in the Senate. Though with little true power, the Senate holds some influence over the People, if not the War Council.<br/><br/>Scythes<br/>1) One of the many peasant volunteer auxiliaries pit against heretical knights, traitor Legions and Godless hordes of the Empire's enemies, you flee the military after seeing too much unnecessary blood being shed on both sides. Thinking to seek refuge at your home village, you return only to see your village razed by Imperial soldiers. Tears in your eyes, you swear vengeance and death to the Empire.<br/><br/>2) Terrified by the power vested in the Emperor's Legions, you - a legionnaire yourself, die a little inside as you are forced to murder women and children in order to put down suspected rebel camps and cells. You know the truth though. The Emperor's terror campaign is merely aimed to keep the people eternally loyal to Him, by steel of sword. His tyranny must end.SGS2) You were part of the Queen and the Warmaster's rebellion. Sometime during the Heresy, your forces accompanied the Queen into the catacombs of an Imperial Temple. You were assaulted by warp-spawn and was left to die with the Queen... except that you didn't die. You have been imbued with the power of Chaos. Unsure of what you truly are, you begin to dig your way out to the surface..<br/><br/>3) You are one of the Custodian Guard of the Emperor. You are, in truth, a fragment of the Emperor's spiritual might. You are a Paladin. Wielding your mighty hammer, you have slain many in His name... but a darkness has come upon the Emperor and during this time of crisis He has become moody, and brooding. The weight of the Crown is too much it seems, for the God-Emperor. With the Heir Apparent in the hands of the Warmaster, it seems it falls to your Order.. or at least you, to dispose of the Emperor, so that His Eternal Light will be free to destroy the enemies of the Empire! Sure... you're killing the Emperor... but it's for Great Justice so that makes it okay.<br/><br/>Silas<br/>1) You are a petty lord of an insignificant hilltop keep, whose job is to merely produce crops for the Emperor's armies. You grew tired of your existence... and wish for more.<br/><br/>2) The Cult of Drakon has spread its tendrils far into the Empire, thanks to the continued efforts of missionary-agents. You are a Magos of the Cult, sent to infiltrate the Capital and destroy all resistance to the Cult, namely - The cult of the God-Emperor..<br/><br/>3) You are an agent of the Black Knights, an order of warriors intent on slaying the Emperor. Your mission begins with you slipping inside the Capital and monitoring the security detail, its numbers, strength and frequency..<br/><br/>Thegreatergood<br/>1) You are a merchant of the Empire, and your goods have recently been 'donated' to the war effort. Almost nearly dry and run to the ground by the official thievery, you pool whatever resources that you have to strike at the person in charge... You really liked that tiara.<br/><br/>2) You are a junior clerk of the War Council, whose job is to give tactical and strategic input which no one really listens to. You are frustrated with the Generals and the Emperor, whose battle plans are mostly something along the lines of 'kill ALL the things!" The Empire will not survive with these idiots in charge...<br/><br/>3) You're a Wizard, Harry. You come from across the Sea from the land of the Elves. And as they all say - "I COME TO CLEANSE THIS LAND."<br/><br/>SGS<br/><br/>18th January 2012, 07:53<br/>Knight-Seneschal Arceon Kathro, Champion of the 17th legion.<br/><br/>scythes<br/><br/>18th January 2012, 11:58Former Captain Sir Ronin De Polo, now a member of the feared Black Knights, refused to allow such an evil as the current King continue to plague the land.<br/><br/>Silas<br/><br/>18th January 2012, 17:01<br/><br/>3) You are an agent of the Black Knights, an order of warriors intent on slaying the Emperor. While there are many operatives tasked for killing the Emperor, they do so independently and from various points of entry. You are given the dank sewers of the Capital as your domain. What will be your first course of action?<br/><br/>1. Stock up on some supplies, get a sword, and begin your journey into the depths.<br/><br/>2. You will need help. Go and find those who were once loyal to you before you begin.<br/><br/>3. Request a team of Black Knights to follow you. (The difference between 2 and 3 is that 2 will lead you to a mini story trying to find your old soldiers, and 3 will just give you a team to work on)<br/><br/>Silas<br/>Months of passed. Battle lines change and move. Armies live and die, but your eyes are forever trained on the Palace and their guards. You feel that at long last, you are ready.<br/><br/>1. |  so we're going to leave off where we were last time talking about buffers. And I'm a big fan of buffers, actually, and buffers are extremely useful in my research because they keep the p h of things constant. So if you're going to do any kind of biology research or biochemistry research, you need to know about buffers. And as I'll mentioned later, also your body needs to be buffered appropriately, you don't want a large changes in p h in your body or things won't function properly. So buffering is very important.<br/><br/>So let's go over buffers. So if we talk about an acid buffer, that's something that's buffering on the acidic side of the p h range. And in that, you want to have play on both sides. So if you have a strong acid, it can be neutralized, the p h stays the same. If you had a strong base, that would be neutralized so the p h stays the same. So what happens, you have to have an acid and its conjugate base in the mixture to have a good buffer.<br/><br/>So what the weak acid is going to do is it will transfer protons or hydrogen ions to hydroxide ions that are supplied by the strong base to neutralize that strong base that was added. So you need to have an acid in there that's capable of giving up a proton to neutralize the hydroxide ion that was added. You also need to have a conjugate base. The conjugate base is going to accept protons if an acid is added, thereby neutralizing the effect of that added acid. So again, you need a weak acid and its conjugate base, you need both to have a good buffer.<br/><br/>So I've been talking about weak acids and weak conjugate bases, so why would a strong acid and the salt of its conjugate base not make a good buffer? What do strong acids do? They dissociate almost completely, and that's not going to do you any good. You want to be able to shift that equation both directions. So a strong acid goes to completion is going to drive it to the right, and for a good buffer you want to be able to switch things around that if you add a strong acid, then you neutralize that, you add a strong base, you neutralize it. So you need the conjugate to be effective as a base, and a strong acid has an ineffective conjugate base. It's not good as a base at all, so that won't work. So buffers are made up of weak acids and their conjugate bases, or weak bases and their conjugate acids. They need to be in the weak range or there's no buffering capacity.<br/><br/>So let's look at a base buffer example. The only difference here is that a basic buffer is going to buffer on the basic end of the p h range. So here's an equation. We have a base in water forming a conjugate of that weak base and hydroxide ions. So let's think about what happens if a strong acid is added. Well if a strong acid is added, then this base, n h 3, can accept protons from the incoming acid and make more n h 4 plus, thereby removing the strong acid from the solution and neutralizing the p h.<br/><br/>If on the other hand a strong base is added, n h 4 plus or ammonium ions can donate the proton forming its conjugate base in water, and again, the p h remains about the same. So the base and its conjugate acid can both react, neutralizing the p h.<br/><br/>So in the base buffer acid, a weak base, b, will accept protons supplied by the strong acid, removing a strong acid from the solution. The conjugate acid of that weak base, which again, would be a weak acid, can transfer protons to the hydroxide ions added by the strong base, again, neutralizing the effect there.<br/><br/>So a buffer is a mixture of a weak acid base conjugate pair that will stabilize the p h serving as a source or a sink for the added protons. So that's how a buffer works.<br/><br/>So, I mentioned buffering is very important. It's very important in your blood. Your blood is buffered in the range of p h 7 . 35 to 7 . 45, and you have a buffering pair and a conjugate acid base pair that's in your blood that does to work. So nature has its own buffering system design to keep the p h of your blood constant.<br/><br/>Well what happens if the p h of your blood changes? So let me just tell you about one disease that can change the p h of your blood. This is from a vitamin B12 dependent enzyme called methylmalonic coa mutase, and this enzyme is your friend. I think that most of you are excited about the idea of having all your fat go to energy, so you should be all fan of this enzyme. So it breaks down from fatty acids -- you get methylmalonyl coa, which is converted to succinyl coa and goes into the citric acid cycle. But if this step is blocked, if there's some kind of genetic disease associated with that particular enzyme, you get a condition called methylmalonic aciduria.<br/><br/>So this is excreted in the body, it can't be converted to the succinyl coa, so it has to be excreted from the body, and it has an acidic p h. And so when it's excreted, it will actually, there's a large amount of it, and it can change the p h of blood. So even though you have this buffering system, it can overwhelm the buffering system and cause a change in the p h, which can lead to neurological disorders and sometimes death.<br/><br/>So they can look for this in newborns, they can do a test, and I was -- one of the tests is -- it's not in every state, but it is in Massachusetts, so my daughter, Samantha, had this test. But they can simply look at the urine of a newborn and look at the p h and whether any of this is being excreted. And some infants that have this condition can excrete like a gram of this acid a day. So it's really large quantities of acid that are excreted and it goes beyond the buffering capacity.<br/><br/>So, I'm going to tell you a little story and it sort of emphasizes some of the importance of genetic research. So before the human genome project, scientists were trying to find the gene to figure out what was wrong with these patients that had this condition. And here are all the amino acids translated from the gene. This is not a small protein, that's a lot of amino acids. And they found that the problem was right here, or one of the main problems is right here. There's a glycine residue, which glycine is the smallest amino acid and that had been changed to something else and that led to the disease.<br/><br/>Now I want you to imagine if you were parents of a small child and you found out your child had this condition and then you had heard that there were advances, they now know what caused it, and so, they went to the doctor and the doctor said, yes, your child, the DNA is wrong, and so let's substitute a different amino acid for glycine here. OK. What does that do for you? Like why is that a problem? Well, they didn't know why it was a problem, they just knew that it had the wrong amino acid in there.<br/><br/>And so, the next step was to try to understand what was actually going on. So what happened in this case was that the vitamin B12 cofactor had changed its conformation when it bound to the protein. And instead of having this sort of loop structure here, it had more of a tail. So this base here had moved down, and so there was a different shape to the vitamin than had been expected.<br/><br/>So when the first structure was solved of one of these enzymes, it found that here's kind of what the vitamin B12 looked like, it had this extended region which no one was expecting, and so that had to fit into the protein. So this is the domain of the protein that binds the vitamin, and it had a large hole, and you put these together and that's great, it fits and you have happy enzyme. Well, how do you create a hole -- nature supposedly abhors a vacuum, so how do you have this hole? Well, what nature did was it put the smallest amino acids there are, glycines, right along here to make room for this to fit together.<br/><br/>So, what was happening in this genetic case is that the switch of a glycine to an argenine did this to the hole. So, what people were trying to do is treat these patients with extra B12, you give them injections of it, you have a lot more B12, and you want to the B12 to bind and get the activity back going again. But really, no matter how much vitamin B12 you added to it, it wasn't really going to do much good, it just wasn't going to fit.<br/><br/>So instead, there was a suggestion that instead of giving the whole vitamin, perhaps you could just give a truncated version of the vitamin, which was commercially available, and that that might bind and restore activity of the protein. So this is what was suggested once you knew what the problem actually was.<br/><br/>So this is just an example of a genetic disease, and it's not that common, but as I said, it is tested for here in Massachusetts. And how knowing something about what the problem is can lead to a potential solution. But for most of you, your blood is doing just fine, you don't have a genetic condition which is pumping too much acid into your bloodstream, so that nature's buffering capacity is working quite well.<br/><br/>So buffers are important. It's great to have my important statements emphasized up there. We've been rehearsing at home. OK. So let's do a sample buffer problem. All right, suppose we have an acid, 0.1 moles of an acid, and 0.5 moles of its conjugate base added in the form of a salt. And so they're put into water and diluted to 1.0 liter. And you're given information about the k a of the acid, 1 . 77 times 10 to the minus 4, and you're asked to calculate the p h. So what do you do?<br/><br/>All right, first, it's always good to write the equation that you're talking about. And so you have acid in water forming hydronium ions and the conjugate base. So here, the acid is giving up a proton to the water, forming h 3 o plus and it's conjugate base.<br/><br/>Now we want to think about what happens at equilibrium. So we know how many moles we have and we know what the total volume is. And in this table we should be using molarity, but the math here is pretty easy because we have 1 mole and 1 liter or 1, and 0.5 moles in 1 liter or 0.5 for molarity. And now at equilibrium what's going to happen, well, when this equilibrates some of this will go away and more of these are going to be formed.<br/><br/>So we have minus x over here, plus x and plus x over there, and we add it all together you get 1 minus x plus x and 0.5 plus x. So the important thing to remember is in buffering problems, you have the acid and the conjugate. So we're used to seeing these tables where we only have something over here and it's all zero on the other side. But in a buffering problem, you have things on both sides from the very beginning. And that's a really important point, and that alone, if you can remember that, will get you a long way through this unit. So you remember that in a buffer you got to have both an acid in its conjugate base or a base in its conjugate acid.<br/><br/>So here we're talking about an acid in water, it's an acidic buffer, and so we can use our k a value -- we want to know what the concentrations are at equilibrium to calculate our p h. So we can use our k a and we can set it up, so we have on the top products over reactants, and we we're not including water in this because it's dilute in solution and so it's not changing very much. And now we can plug in our values, so we have 0.5 plus x, x over 1 minus x.<br/><br/>All right, now this is just written again from the last slide, we can try an approximation, which is it that x is small compared to 1 molar or 0.5 molar. And so we can get rid of the plus x and the minus x down here and just have one x term, which makes it simpler to solve, but we'll have to go back and check that approximation in a few minutes.<br/><br/>So making that approximation, we can calculate x as 3 . 54 times 10 to the minus 4 molar, and now we need to check that assumption. Well, you can probably guess it's going to be OK, because something times 10 to the minus 4 compared to 0.5 and 1, that's probably going to be OK that it's small. But our assumption here is that it needs to be less than 5%, and here it's 0.1%. So you could just take this value of x, divide it by the smaller of the two, 0.5 , times 100, and calculate the percent ionization. If it's less than 5% you're OK, if it's more you need to use the quadratic equation to solve the problem.<br/><br/>So x here is our concentration of hydronium ion, which is great, because then we can easily calculate the p h. So p h again is minus log of the hydronium ion concentration, and so the p h here is 3 . 45. And again, significant figures, we'll be talking about this as we go through. The volume had two, it was 1 . liters, and so we're going to have two significant figures after the decimal point, because the volume was limiting insignificant figures.<br/><br/>All right, now let's consider what happens if some strong acid had been added in the solution. So the volume is still going to be 1 liter, because the acid was added in before we went up to the 1 liter mark.<br/><br/>So strong acids, they go to completion. So, if we added 0.1 moles of that strong acid, it will react with equal number of moles of the conjugate base to form the conjugate acid. So we can just do subtractions here. We don't have to worry about setting up any kind of equilibrium, we assume it goes completely.<br/><br/>So for the conjugate base we had 0.5 moles before, and it's going to be reacting then with a strong acid, so 0.1 of those will react, leaving us with 0.4 moles of the conjugate, and that's in 1 liter. So now we have 0.4 molar. For the acid, we had 1 mole to begin with, but now we formed more as they reaction has taken place, and so we have 0.1 more. Now we have 1 . 1 moles again in 1 liter.<br/><br/>So now we can do the same thing again. So after this has happened, after we have added the strong acid, then a new equilibrium is going to be reached. And so we can plug in this table as well. So we have 1 . 1 now over here, and 0.4 on the other side. As equilibrium is approached, you lose some of the acid as it reacts with water and ionizes and you form more of the h 3 o plus and more of the conjugate. So we have 1 . 1 minus x, x, and 0.4 plus x.<br/><br/>So again, the trick here is just to remember that there is a reaction with a strong acid that has been added, and you need to figure out the new molarities, and then go back to your equilibrium table with those new molarities.<br/><br/>So here we can set up with a k a again, and do the same problem that we did before. And so, we can make the assumption again that x is small, try it out and see if it works. X turns out to be 4 . 87 times 10 to the minus 4 molars -- so again, it's a small number. And so, it turns out to be 1 . -- less than 1% of 0.4 . If it's less than 5% of the smaller number, you don't have to worry about the bigger number, and so your assumption is OK, you don't need to use the quadratic equation. And so, then we can calculate at the end that the p h is now 3 . 31, again, two significant figures after the decimal place because of the volume. So addition of 0.1 moles of a strong acid changed our p h from 3 . 45 to 3 . 31. So we buffered pretty well. There was a change in p h, we added acid and the p h did go down, but not by an enormous amount. And so, that's because this turned out to be a pretty decent buffer here, so we didn't have a really huge effect.<br/><br/>So, those are -- that's an example of a buffer problem.<br/><br/>So, let's think for a minute about designing a buffer. Suppose you wanted to create a buffer at a particular p h, what do you need to think about? Well, you need to think about the ratio of your acid to the conjugate, and you need to think about the p k a of the acid, and the p h that's desired.<br/><br/>So here is a generic equation for an acid in water, so we have our acid, h a, in water forming hydronium ions and our conjugate base. And now we're going to do a little derivation to come up with an equation that would be useful to you in thinking about buffers and designing buffers.<br/><br/>So, we can write a generic term for k a. K a equals products over reactants, and so we have our hydronium ions are conjugate base over our acid. And now we can take this term and rearrange it. So we can pull a hydronium ion concentration over to one side. And now we're going to take the logs of both sides, so we get the log of hydronium ion concentration, the log of k a, and the log of h a concentration over a minus concentration, and now we're going to multiply everything by negative sign, so we have minus logs of things. And I'm just going to move this now up to the top of the screen, and we have this equation.<br/><br/>So what is minus log of hydronium ion concentration? P h. What's minus log of k a? P k a. So, we have p h equals p k a minus the log of a concentration of your acid over the concentration of your conjugate base. And these are equilibrium concentrations for that, because remember, we derived it from our equilibrium expression for k a. But a lot of times when you're working buffer problems, you know the concentrations that you added of these things, not necessarily the concentrations of equilibrium.<br/><br/>And if we rewrite this expression in terms of the initial concentrations or original or o concentrations, then we have p h is approximately equal to p k a minus log of the initial concentrations of your acid over your conjugate base. And this is known as a Henderson Hasselbalch equation. And people love the Henderson Hasselbalch equation, and it can be incredibly useful to you in solving these problems. But I'm going to emphasize when it's OK to use it and when it's not OK to use it, because people try to apply it to everything, and it's for buffer problems, and people apply it to all sorts of things that are not buffer problems. So I want to make sure that it's clear when you can use it and when you can't use it. And it's fine to use it and you should use it when it's acceptable, but not at other times. It's one of the few equations in acid base, so people get very excited by it.<br/><br/>All right, so remember that it's really equal when they're at equilibrium concentrations, and this is just an approximation that we're saying that those are initial concentrations. So when is it OK to say well, the equilibrium concentration is more or less the same as the initial concentration. And that's true when x, which is in this particular example your hydronium ion concentration, is small compared to your initial concentration of the acid and the conjugate base that you added to the buffer. So, and when x is small, then pretty much, the equilibrium concentration equals the initial concentration. So there's not really very much change if x is a really small number.<br/><br/>So, a lot of the time this is true. Remember, we're making buffers from weak acids and weak conjugate bases, and the definition of a weak acid is that it only loses a tiny fraction of its protons. It only ionizes slightly in water. And a weak base typically only accepts a fraction of the protons it can accept. It's a weak base, so it's not doing a whole lot of chemistry there. So this approximation is good a lot of the time. So just you know when you can and can not use it, we're going to give a rule and say it's the same rule as before. When we say when x is small or your hydronium ion concentration is small compared to the acid or the conjugate base, when it's less than 5%, that's what we're going to call small, so the same rule we've been using all along. When x is small, this approximation works pretty well. And you can plug in your concentrations right there -- your initial concentrations not your equilibrium concentrations.<br/><br/>All right, so let's use Henderson Hasselbalch and design a buffer system if we want a p h of 4 . 6. And a good rule to remember is that a buffering solution is most effective when it's plus or minus 1 away from the p k a. So that's a good thing too I keep in mind for research as well. If you're really far away from the p k a, it's not going to be a good buffering system, and this is a mistake the people make in the lab sometimes, so you may run into that in some of your laboratory courses.<br/><br/>So we want a buffer about p h 4 . 6. So we can look at our ionization constants of acid, and of course, we're always doing everything at room temperature in this unit, and so we can look at what p k a's are of some weak acids, and acetate, acetate is an easy buffer to get your hands on, acetate is quite available, and it has a good p k a 4 . 75. So that's great, we can use that in designing our buffer. So, acetic acid has the right p k a, and we're all set, we can prepare a buffer. But we'll need to add the acid and the conjugate, and we need to know how much acid and how much of the conjugate to add.<br/><br/>So we can use Henderson Hasselbalch equation for this. We're creating a buffer, and so this is an equation we can use for buffers. We know what p h we want, we know the p k a of the thing we're going to use, and we want to figure out how much of the acid to use and how much of the conjugate to use. We need to know the ratio of one to the other to set up our buffer ideally. So we can plug our numbers in and calculate that.<br/><br/>So, we can rearrange this if you want so that the unknown is on one side and we have to p k a and the p h on the other side. And so, we have here our p k a minus our p h and we get . 15. Now we need to inverse log and come up with the ratio, and the ratio that we want is 1 . 4. So we want to have a ratio from our acid to the conjugate of 1 . 4.<br/><br/>Well how much exactly are we going to add them, we know the ratio now, how much are we going to add? Well, you could use 1 . 4 molar and 1 molar, for example, that would have the correct ratio. But how do you know if that's going to be good? Well, the ratio is more important than the exact amounts, however, the amounts get to this issue of sort of the capacity of the buffer -- how resistant it is to changes in the p h. So if you use too low concentrations of both, it won't be all that resistant. So if you need to have a better buffering capacity, the higher concentrations that you should use. And you can use Henderson Hasselbalch then to calculate what sort of the minimum amount you use, the minimum amount to have that 5% rule work.<br/><br/>So in the example I told you about this condition, the buffer in the blood was pretty good, but the acid with so much that it overwhelmed buffering capacity. So if there had been larger concentrations of the buffer, that would have certainly helped in that case. So the higher the concentrations you use, the more resistant to change. And that's the idea of buffering capacity. So, and if you use too low concentrations, then your Henderson Hasselbalch equation won't even be valid. So we can go back and calculate what is sort of the minimum concentrations we need to use for Henderson Hasselbalch to be valid to meet that 5% rule. So for a p h of 4 . 6, a hydronium ion concentration is a 2 . 5 times 10 to the minus 5.<br/><br/>And so then if we work our equation backwards, we want to be less than 5%, so we have this over the concentration of either one of those times 100% gives you 5%. So the concentrations need to be greater than 5 times 10 to the minus 4 moles or you won't meet this 5% rule. So at least to be greater than that and they need to be in the right ratio. Other than that you're somewhat free to decide what you're going to do with that, and there may be other considerations involved if you're working with a certain concentration of protein, you might not want your buffer concentration to be too high, it might start interfering with enzyme assay, for example. And often buffers are somewhere around 100 millimolar, that's a pretty common concentration for buffers.<br/><br/>All right, so this slide we're doing really well. So let's talk about titration, so that as soon as class is over today, you can go work on those questions on the problem-set.<br/><br/>All right. So acid base titrations. How many of you in high school titrated an acid with a base? Large fraction of you, OK. So usually what titration problems are meant to do, that you have something known about an acid or a base, base say of known concentration, and and acid of unknown concentration or maybe unknown molecular weight, and you're titrating them out, titrating them together to find missing information. So you can determine concentration, sometimes you can determine a molecular weight.<br/><br/>All right, so here's what a plot looks like and some of the key terms involved in acid base titrations. So you have a p h on one side, and then volume of either base or acid added on the other side. So p h versus a volume. And the actual experiment here, you have a base that you're dripping, one drip at a time supposedly into your strong acid. And then you're looking to figure out when you're going to reach the either equivalence point or the end point, these are basically the same terms. It's also called the stoichiometric point, and often equivalence point is used as a more theoretical amount of volume is added, where as end point is the experimentally measured. So we're going to be talking a lot about equivalence points, because we have no lab associated with this course, so everything will be theorectical in terms of these, but they should be the same.<br/><br/>So, many times you will measure p h with the p h meter -- this just shows a p h meter. And for those of you who have done these experiments, this should look familiar. And for those of you who haven't, often what you're doing when you're dripping something in, you're waiting for an indicator dye to change color as an indication that you have reached the end point, and often you're adding, and it's clear, clear, clear for what seems to be forever, and just as you get incredibly impatient, you start adding it faster and you go all the way to this dark color. And what you want is this very, very, very light changed color that indicates the end point. So you usually go too slow and then go too fast and then have to do the experiment over again.<br/><br/>Those of you who know how to calculate theoretical values could sit down and do a calculation first and then go really fast right until you get around this point and add it really slowly, and be done with lab sooner. So, we'll talk about how you do the theoretical value, which could save you a lot of time if you ever run across these labs in any future class.<br/><br/>So, here are the two curves that you would see for either a strong acid with a strong base, or strong base with a strong acid. So if you're titrating a strong acid with a strong base, you're going to start down being very acidic, because all you have is your strong acid. And then as you add in base, the p h will go up. You'll reach this point -- s is for the stoichiometric point, we also call this, again, the equivalence point, and then it'll continue to go up and then start to level off a bit more up here.<br/><br/>If you're going the other direction, you're starting with strong base, your p h is going to be high, and the p h will decrease as you add the acid. And then you get to the stoichiometric or equivalence point in the middle of this curve here. And then as you go down farther, more acidic, it starts to level off. So those are what the titration curves look like.<br/><br/>So let's do an example. We're going to have a strong base being titrated with a strong acid first. And our strong base is n a o h, and our strong acid is h c l. So let's calculate the p h at the equivalence point. Well, let's just calculate the p h at 5 mils -- I don't know whether it's equivalence point, actually. 5 mils of this have been added to the amount of base. So we have 25 mils of our strong base at 0.15 molar concentration, and we're adding 5 mils of our 0.34 molar acid.<br/><br/>So what we want to do is figure out how many moles of base we had to start with. So how many moles of o h minus -- again, n a o h is a strong base, so however much n a o h you add, is the amount of o h minus you get, and so we just put in the number of liters times the concentration to get moles. We don't need any equilibrium table here.<br/><br/>So then we want to figure out the amount of acid added when you're adding 5 mils of it. So again, h c l is a strong acid, so the amount of h c l added equals the amount of h 3 o plus, that's formed, goes to completion, and so we added 5 mils, the concentration was 0.34 molar, so we have 1 . 7 times 10 to the minus 3 moles.<br/><br/>So now, the strong acid is going to react with the strong base, and we want to figure out how much of the base is left, how much hydroxide ion is left after it reacts with the hydronium ions. So it'll react 1:1, so we had 6 . 25 times 10 to the minus 3 moles, and we added 1 . 7 times 10 to the minus 3 moles of the acid, so we're going to have 4 . 55 times 10 the minus 3 moles left. And now we can calculate the molarity here, so we have the number of moles and the new volume, so we had 25 mils to the base to begin with. We've added 5 mils to the acid, so our new volume is 30, and so we have our new concentration.<br/><br/>And now we can calculate p h. First we'll calculate p o h, and then use 14 minus to get the new p h. So if you were making your own little titration curve, we could have volume of acid on one side and p h on the other side, and you have a point of 13 . 18. That's how much we have after you have 5 mils added. So we didn't, in this curve, we have not measured the zero point, but we know what one point after 5 mils of the acid has been added.<br/><br/>So now let's go right to the equivalence point -- so the way I draw it should indicate that was not an equivalence point. So we want to figure out how much we need to add of the acid to reach the equivalence point. So the equivalence point or the stoichiometric point means that you add the same amount of moles of your titrate as you had, and so they're going to be equal to each other. So if you're adding acid to a strong base, you've added enough acid that you now have equal number of moles to the number of moles of base that you had to begin with. So we know we have 6 . 25 times 10 to the minus 3 moles of the base were present.<br/><br/>So at the equivalence point, you need to have that exact same number of moles of acid. So we know the concentration of the acid we have and the number of moles we need, and so we can calculate the volume, which is 18 . 4 millimeters, or 0.0184 liters. So what would the p h then be of the equivalence point? We're titrating a strong acid with a strong base, what should the p h be? The p h should be 7. So somewhere around here we're going to be at 18, so you can just try to draw a curve, and here we have a p h of 7 when we've gone to about 18 . 4 milliliters added.<br/><br/>So when you titrate a strong acid with a strong base, you form a salt that's neutral. Because the conjugates of a strong acid and a strong base are not basic or acidic. They're ineffective as acids or bases, so you're going to get a neutral salt. So if you're doing a problem with a strong acid and strong base, it's not a lot of calculations that need to be done at this point, you just need to recognize that your salt should be neutral.<br/><br/>So now, we can talk about what happens if you add an extra milliliter of your acid, after you've reached that equivalence point, which is something that people probably have done in doing these titrations not meaning to, gone well beyond the equivalence point.<br/><br/>So first we can find out the number of moles of the strong acid that we've added extra, so we added one mil of the strong acid, and so again, it goes to completion. The concentration, the acid, is 0.34 molar times our 1 mil, so we've added 3 . 4 times 10 to the minus 4 extra moles of our acid, and so let's calculate then what the molarity is that we have added that was extra.<br/><br/>And even if you don't have a calculator, we've helped you out. Let's just take 10 more seconds.<br/><br/>I think this is a first, isn't it? Did I mention it's a good idea to start the problem-set early. So the trick here was about the volume. So we had 25 mils to begin with, then we added 18 . 4 to get to the equivalence point, and then we're 1 mil beyond the equivalence point. And the tricks to these parts of the problems are to remember all the things that have been added to get to the volume.<br/><br/>So the problem itself is not very tricky of having just an acid in water, but the one trick is in calculating the molarity, remembering all of the things that were added to get to this point. And so by the end of the problem-set this should be pretty familiar to you, but it's also something that you want to make sure that you check on an exam that you're not making any volume mistakes.<br/><br/>So again, here are the things that you want to remember to add in in calculating this. And then, if you calculate the p h of that, you get a p h of 2 . 1106, so that's down somewhere in here. We've added 1 more mill, and we're at a p h of 2 . 1106, and this can be a check as well. Always ask yourself when you have a strong base and you're adding acid, before you've added very much, your p h should be basic. Mar sin má tá tú ag dul a dhéanamh ar aon chineál taighde bitheolaíochta nó taighde bithcheimiceach, ní mór duit fios a bheith agat faoi mhaoláin. Agus mar a luafaidh mé níos déanaí, ní mór do chorp a chothú go cuí, níl tú ag iarraidh athruithe móra a dhéanamh i bp in do chorp nó ní fheidhmeoidh rudaí i gceart. Mar sin tá mhaolánaithe an-tábhachtach.<br/><br/>Mar sin, a ligean ar dul thar maolánacha.Mar sin má labhraímid faoi maolán aigéad, sin rud éigin a mhaolaíonn ar thaobh aigéadach an raon p. Agus ina sin, ba mhaith leat a bheith ag imirt ar an dá thaobh. Mar sin, má tá aigéad láidir agat, is féidir é a neodrú, fanann an p h mar an gcéanna.Má bhí bonn láidir agat, rud a a a neodrú ionas go bhfanfaidh an p h mar an gcéanna. Mar sin, cad a tharlaíonn, caithfidh tú go mbeadh aigéad agus a bhonn conjulage sa mheascán go mbeadh maolán maith agat.<br/><br/>Mar sin, cad é an aigéad lag atá le déanamh ná go n-aistreoidh sé prótón nó iain hidrigine chuig iain hiodrocsaíde a sholáthraíonn an bonn láidir chun an bonn láidir sin a neodrú a cuireadh leis. Mar sin ní mór duit aigéad a bheith agat ann atá in ann prótón a thabhairt chun an ian hiodrocsaíde a cuireadh leis a neodrú.Ní mór duit bonn conjugulate a bheith agat freisin. Tá bonn an tsughaidh ag dul chun glacadh le prótón má chuirtear aigéad leis, rud a a chuireann éifeacht an aigéid bhreise sin isteach leis.Mar sin arís, teastaíonn aigéad lag agus a bhonn conjulate, ní mór duit maolán maith a bheith agat araon.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá mé ag caint faoi aigéid lag agus bunanna conjutag lag, mar sin cén fáth a mbeadh aigéad láidir agus salann a bhonn conjutage nach bhfuil a dhéanamh maolán maith? Cad a dhéanann siad aigéid láidre a dhéanamh beagnach go hiomlán, agus nach bhfuil ag dul a dhéanamh tú aon mhaith. Ba mhaith leat a bheith in ann an dá threo cothroma sin a athrú. Mar sin, a aigéad láidir dul chun críche ag dul a thiomáint ar an gceart, agus do mhaolán maith is mian leat a bheith in ann rudaí a athrú thart go má chuireann tú aigéad láidir, ansin neodrú tú sin, cuir bonn láidir, a neodrú tú é. Mar sin, is gá duit an t- conjuntagate a bheith éifeachtach mar bhonn, agus tá aigéad láidir bonn conjutage tógálach., Níl sé go maith mar bhonn ar chor ar bith, ionas nach mbeidh a bheith ag obair. Mar sin tá Maoláin comhdhéanta de aigéid laga agus a mbonn conjugulate, nó bunanna lag agus a n-aigéid conjulage.Ní mór dóibh a bheith sa raon lag nó níl aon acmhainn maolánach ann.<br/><br/>Mar sin, a ligean ar breathnú ar sampla Maolán bonn.Is é an t-aon difríocht anseo go bhfuil Maolán bunúsach ag dul a mhaolán ar bhunchríoch an raon p. Mar sin, anseo tá an chothromóid.Tá bonn againn i uisce a chruthaíonn comhcholáiste den bhonn lag agus de na hiain hiodrocsaíde sin. Mar sin, déanaimis smaoineamh ar cad a tharlaíonn má chuirtear aigéad láidir leis. Bhuel má tá aigéad láidir seo a leanas, ansin an bonn, n h 3, is féidir, glacadh le prótón ón aigéad ag teacht isteach agus níos mó n h 4 móide a dhéanamh, rud a bhaint as an aigéad láidir as an réiteach agus neutralizing an p h.<br/><br/>Má chuirtear bonn láidir ar an láimh eile, is féidir le n 4 h nó iain amóiniam an prótón atá ina bhonn conjugulate in uisce, agus arís, fanann an p h mar an gcéanna.Mar sin is féidir leis an bonn agus a aigéad conjutage araon freagairt, neutralizing an p h.<br/><br/>Mar sin, sa bhonn aigéad maolánach, beidh bonn lag, b, glacadh le prótón a sholáthraíonn an aigéad láidir, a bhaint aigéad láidir ón réiteach. Is féidir le haigéad conjulage den bhonn lag sin, a bheadh ina aigéad lag, prótóin a aistriú chuig na hiain hiodrocsaíde a chuir an bonn láidir, arís, neodrú an éifeacht ann.<br/><br/>Mar sin is meascán de bhonn coimeádán aigéad lag a chobhsú ar an p h ag fónamh mar fhoinse nó doirteal do na prótón breise.Mar sin tá go conas a oibríonn Maolán.<br/><br/>Mar sin, luaigh mé go bhfuil maolánach an-tábhachtach.Tá d’fhuil an-tábhachtach i d’fhuil maol i raon p. 7 go 7. 45, agus tá péire maolánaithe agat agus péire bun aigéad conjulage atá i d’fhuil a bheith ag obair. Mar sin tá a dhearadh córas maolánach féin ag an dúlra chun p a choinneáil ar tairiseach d’fhuil.<br/><br/>Bhuel cad a tharlaíonn má an p d'athruithe fola?, Mar sin in iúl dom ach a insint duit faoi cheann amháin ar féidir athrú ar an p h de do chuid fola., Tá sé seo ó einsím B12 dedent ar a dtugtar sómaloic, agus is é an einsím do chara. Sílim go bhfuil an chuid is mó de tú ar bís mar gheall ar an smaoineamh go bhfuil do saill go léir a théann chun fuinnimh, mar sin ba chóir duit a bheith ar fad lucht leanúna an einsím seo. Mar sin, briseann sé síos ó aigéid sailleacha -- gheobhaidh tú coa meitilmonyl, a thiontú go coa sancunyl agus a théann isteach sa timthriall aigéad citreach. Ach má tá an chéim seo bac, má tá roinnt de chineál galar géiniteach a bhaineann leis an einsím áirithe sin, gheobhaidh tú coinníoll ar a dtugtar aigéadauriamalónach.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá sé seo eisfheartha i gcorp, ní féidir é a thiontú go dtí an coa succinyl, agus mar sin tá sé a bheith eisfhearadh ón gcomhlacht, agus mar sin tá sé ag p. Agus mar sin nuair a tá sé eisfhearadh, beidh sé i ndáiríre, tá méid mór de sé, agus is féidir é a athrú ar an p h de fola. Mar sin, cé go bhfuil an córas maolánach seo agat, féadann sé an córas maolánach a shárú agus athrú a chur ar an p h, rud a d’fhéadfadh neamhoird néareolaíocha a bheith mar thoradh air agus uaireanta bás.<br/><br/>Mar sin, is féidir leo lorg seo i leanaí nuabheirthe, is féidir leo a dhéanamh le tástáil, agus bhí mé - tá sé i ngach stát, ach tá sé i Massachusetts, mar sin mo iníon, Samantha, go raibh an tástáil. Ach is féidir leo breathnú go simplí ar an fual de nuabheirthe agus breathnú ar an p h agus cibé an bhfuil aon cheann de seo á gcomhlíonadh., Agus roinnt naíonán go bhfuil an coinníoll is féidir a exeuige seo cosúil le graim den aigéad seo in aghaidh an lae. Mar sin, tá sé i ndáiríre cainníochtaí móra d'aigéad go bhfuil eisfhearadh agus téann sé níos faide ná an cumas maolánach.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá mé ag dul a insint duit scéal beag agus cuireann sé an cineál na tábhacht a bhaineann le taighde géiniteach. Mar sin roimh an tionscadal genome daonna, bhí eolaithe ag iarraidh a fháil ar an géine a fháil amach cad a bhí cearr leis na hothair go raibh an coinníoll seo.Agus anseo tá na aimínaigéid go léir a aistríodh ó na géine. Ní próitéin beag é seo, tá go leor aimínaigéid. Agus fuair siad amach go raibh an fhadhb ceart anseo, nó ceann de na príomhfhadhbanna tá ceart anseo. Tá iarmhair ghilfínéiteach ann, arb é glicín an aimínaigéid is lú agus a athraíodh go dtí rud éigin eile agus a raibh an galar mar thoradh air.<br/><br/>Anois ba mhaith liom tú a shamhlú má bhí tú tuismitheoirí de leanbh beag agus fuair tú amach go raibh an riocht seo agus ansin chuala tú go raibh dul chun cinn, tá a fhios acu anois cad ba chúis leis, agus mar sin, chuaigh siad chuig an dochtúir agus an dochtúir a dúirt, tá do leanbh, tá an DNA mícheart, agus mar sin a ligean ar a chur in ionad aimínaigéad difriúil don glicín anseo. Cad a dhéanann ar do shon? Cosúil le cén fáth go bhfuil fadhb? Bhuel, ní raibh a fhios acu cén fáth go raibh sé ina fhadhb, bhí a fhios acu ach go raibh sé an aigéad aimín mícheart ann.<br/><br/>Agus mar sin, ba é an chéad chéim eile ná iarracht a dhéanamh tuiscint a fháil ar a raibh ar siúl i ndáiríre. Mar sin, ba é an rud a tharla sa chás seo ná gur athraigh an conraitheoir vitimín B12 a chomhréireacht nuair a bhí sé faoi cheangal ag an bpróitéin. Agus in ionad an cineál struchtúr lúb seo a bheith agat anseo, bhí níos mó eireaball air.Mar sin bhog an bonn seo anseo síos, agus mar sin bhí cruth difriúil ar an vitimín ná mar a bhí ag súil leis.<br/><br/>Mar sin, nuair a bhí an chéad struchtúr a réiteach ar cheann de na einsímí, fuair sé go bhfuil an cineál seo ar an méid a d'fhéach an vitimín B12 mhaith, go raibh an réigiún leathnaithe nach raibh aon duine ag súil, agus ionas go raibh a d'oirfeadh isteach sa phróitéin. Mar sin is é seo an réimse an próitéin a chónaisc na vitimín, agus bhí sé ina poll mór, agus chuir tú iad seo le chéile agus go bhfuil go hiontach, oireann sé agus tá tú einsím sásta. Bhuel, conas a chruthaíonn tú poll - nádúr is dócha go n-osclófar bhfolús, mar sin conas a bhfuil tú an poll seo? Bhuel, cén cineál a raibh sé a chur ar na aimínaigéid is lú tá, glicíní, ceart chomh maith le seomra a dhéanamh le haghaidh seo a d'oirfeadh le chéile.<br/><br/>Mar sin, is é an rud a bhí ag tarlú sa chás géiniteach seo ná go ndearna athrú glycine go arginine é seo leis an poll. Mar sin, cad a bhí daoine ag iarraidh a dhéanamh ná déileáil leis na hothair le B12 breise, a thabhairt duit instealltaí de, tá tú a lán níos mó B12, agus ba mhaith leat chun an B12 a cheangal agus a fháil ar an ghníomhaíocht ar ais ag dul arís. Ach i ndáiríre, is cuma cé mhéad vitimín B12 a chuir tú leis, ní raibh sé ag dul i ndáiríre a dhéanamh go maith i bhfad, ní raibh sé ag dul a d'oirfeadh.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ina ionad sin, bhí moladh a thabhairt in ionad an vitimín ar fad, b'fhéidir go bhféadfaí tú a thabhairt ach leagan teasctha den vitimín, a bhí ar fáil ar bhonn tráchtála, agus d'fhéadfadh gníomhaíocht an próitéin a cheangal agus a chur ar ais. Mar sin is é seo a mholtar nuair a fhios agat cad a bhí an fhadhb i ndáiríre.<br/><br/>Mar sin níl anseo ach sampla de ghalar géiniteach, agus níl sé chomh coitianta sin, ach mar a dúirt mé, déantar é a thástáil anseo i Massachusetts. Agus conas a fhios agam rud éigin faoi cad is féidir leis an bhfadhb a bheith mar thoradh air réiteach féideartha. Ach don chuid is mó de tú, tá d’fhuil ag déanamh ach fíneáil, níl riocht géiniteach agat atá ag caidéalú aigéad an iomarca i do shruth fola, ionas go mbeidh cumas maolánach an dúlra ag obair go maith.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá buaircíní tábhachtach. Tá sé iontach go bhfuil mo ráitis thábhachtacha béime ann.Tá taighde déanta againn sa bhaile. OK. Mar sin, déanaimis fadhb bhreise samplach a dhéanamh. Tá gach ceart, is dócha go bhfuil aigéad, 0.1 móil d'aigéad, agus 0.5 móil ar a bhonn conjutagre seo a leanas i bhfoirm salann agus mar sin tá siad a chur isteach uisce agus chaolú le 1.0 lítear. Agus tugtar eolas duit faoi na k an aigéad, 1. 77 uair 10 go dtí an lúide 4, agus an bhfuil tú ag iarraidh a ríomh ar an p h. Mar sin, cad a dhéanann tú?<br/><br/>Gach ceart, ar dtús, tá sé go maith i gcónaí a scríobh an chothromóid go bhfuil tú ag caint faoi., Agus mar sin tá tú aigéad in uisce a fhoirmiú iain hydronium agus an bonn conjugrate. Mar sin anseo, is é an aigéad a thabhairt suas le próitéin leis an uisce, ag teacht h 3 o móide agus tá sé bonn conjulage.<br/><br/>Anois ba mhaith linn smaoineamh ar cad a tharlaíonn ag cothromaíochta.Mar sin tá a fhios againn cé mhéad mó mó mó atá againn agus tá a fhios againn cad é an méid iomlán. Agus sa tábla seo ba chóir dúinn a bheith ag baint úsáide as moráltacht, ach tá an math anseo éasca go leor toisc go bhfuil 1 caochÚn agus 1 lítear nó 1, agus 0.5 míle i 1 lítear nó 0.5 le haghaidh gluaiseachta. Agus anois ag cothromaíochta cad atá ag tarlú, go maith, nuair a ardaíonn sé seo beidh roinnt de seo ag dul ar shiúl agus níos mó díobh seo le déanamh.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ní mór dúinn lúide x os cionn anseo, móide x agus móide x os a chionn, agus cuirimid sé go léir le chéile a gheobhaidh tú 1 lúide x móide x agus 0.5 móide x. Mar sin, is é an rud tábhachtach a mheabhrú i fadhbanna maolánacha, tá an t-aigéad agus an t-iondacáit agat. Mar sin, táimid ag úsáid a fheiceáil na táblaí i gcás ina bhfuil againn ach rud éigin thar anseo agus tá sé go léir náid ar an taobh eile., Ach i bhfadhb mhaolaithe, tá tú rudaí ar an dá thaobh as an-tús. Agus is é sin pointe i ndáiríre tábhachtach, agus go ina n-aonar, más féidir leat cuimhneamh go mbeidh, a gheobhaidh tú ar bhealach fada tríd an aonad seo., Mar sin, cuimhin leat go bhfuair tú i mhaolán a fuair tú araon aigéad ina bhonn conjugulate nó bonn ina aigéad conjutagre.<br/><br/>Mar sin, anseo tá muid ag caint faoi aigéad in uisce, tá sé ina maolán aigéadach, agus mar sin is féidir linn a úsáid ár k luach - ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cad iad na tiúchana ag cothromaíochta chun ár p. Mar sin, is féidir linn úsáid a bhaint as ár k a agus is féidir linn a leagtar sé suas, ionas go bhfuil muid ar na táirgí is fearr thar imoibrithe, agus nach bhfuil muid ag áireamh uisce sa mhéid seo toisc go bhfuil sé chaolú i réiteach agus mar sin nach bhfuil sé ag athrú go mór. Agus anois is féidir linn breiseán a dhéanamh inár luachanna, ionas go mbeidh 0.5 móide x, x níos mó ná 1 lúide x.<br/><br/>Gach ceart, anois tá sé seo scríofa ach arís ón sleamhnán seo caite, is féidir linn triail a bhaint as comhfhogasú, a bhfuil sé go bhfuil x beag i gcomparáid le 1 mólar nó 0.5 móilíneach. Agus mar sin is féidir linn fáil réidh leis an móide x agus an lúide x síos anseo agus díreach téarma x amháin, rud a fhágann go bhfuil sé níos simplí a réiteach, ach beidh orainn dul ar ais agus an t-eximpoxation sin a sheiceáil i gceann cúpla nóiméad.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ag déanamh an comhfhogasú sin, is féidir linn x a ríomh mar 3. 54 huaire 10 go dtí an lúide 4 morálta, agus anois is gá dúinn an toimhde sin a sheiceáil. Bhuel, is dócha go mbeidh buille faoi thuairim sé ceart go leor, mar gheall ar amanna rud éigin 10 go dtí an lúide 4 i gcomparáid le 0.5 agus 1, is dócha go mbeidh ceart go leor go bhfuil sé beag. Ach is é ár toimhde anseo go gcaithfidh sé a bheith níos lú ná 5%, agus anseo tá sé 0.1%. Mar sin, d'fhéadfá a ghlacadh ach an luach de x, é a roinnt ag an lú de na dhá, 0.5, amanna 100, agus an inionú faoin gcéad ríomh.Má tá sé níos lú ná 5% tá tú ceart go leor, má tá sé níos mó is gá duit a bhaint as an cothromóid cearnach a réiteach ar an bhfadhb.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá x anseo ár tiúchan ian híoróiniam, atá iontach, mar gheall ar ansin is féidir linn an p.. Mar sin is é p arís logáil lúide an tiúchan ian hidreanium, agus mar sin tá an p h anseo 3. 45. Agus arís, figiúirí suntasacha, beidh muid ag caint faoi seo mar a théann muid tríd. Bhí dhá cheann ag an méid, bhí sé 1 lítear, agus mar sin táimid ag dul go bhfuil dhá fhigiúr suntasacha tar éis an phointe deachúil, toisc go raibh an méid teorainn a figiúirí suntasacha.<br/><br/>I gceart go léir, a ligean ar a mheas cad a tharlaíonn má cuireadh roinnt aigéad láidir sa réiteach.Mar sin tá an méid fós ag dul a bheith 1 lítear, toisc go raibh an aigéad seo a leanas sa sula ndeachaigh muid suas go dtí an 1 marc lítear.<br/><br/>Chomh láidir leis sin, téann siad chun críche.Mar sin, má chuireamar 0.1 mhós den aigéad láidir sin leis, imoibreoidh sé le líon comhionann móil den bhonn conjugulate chun foirm a aigéad conjutage., Mar sin, is féidir linn a dhéanamh ach luaineachtaí anseo. Ní gá dúinn a bheith buartha faoi ar bith cothromaíochta a bhunú, glacadh linn téann sé go hiomlán.<br/><br/>Mar sin, don bhonn conjulate bhí againn 0.5 móil roimh, agus tá sé ag freagairt ansin le aigéad láidir, mar sin 0.1 díobh a bheidh ag freagairt, ag fágáil dúinn le 0.4 moles an conjutage, agus sin i 1 lítear. Mar sin tá muid anois tá 0.4 morálta. Maidir leis an aigéad, bhí againn 1 caochÚn le tosú leis, ach anois déanta againn níos mó agus iad ag freagairt dóibh, agus mar sin tá 0.1 eile againn. Anois tá 1 mhóil againn arís i 1 lítear.<br/><br/>Mar sin anois is féidir linn an rud céanna a dhéanamh arís.Mar sin tar éis seo a tharla, tar éis dúinn an t-aigéad láidir a chur leis, ansin beidh eiquilirium nua ag baint amach.Agus mar sin is féidir linn breiseán a dhéanamh sa tábla seo freisin.Tá 1 os cionn anseo, agus 0.4 ar an taobh eile. Toisc go bhfuil cothromaíocht idir bheag agus mhór, chailleann tú cuid den aigéad mar imoibríonn sé le huisce agus déanann tú iain agus cruthaíonn tú níos mó den móide 3 h agus níos mó den chomhdhonn.Mar sin tá 1 lúide x, x, agus 0.4 móide x.<br/><br/>Mar sin arís, is é an cleas anseo ná cuimhneamh go bhfuil imoibriú ann le haigéad láidir a cuireadh leis, agus ní mór duit na módúlachtaí nua a dhéanamh amach, agus ansin dul ar ais chuig do thábla cothromaíochta leis na módúlachtaí nua sin.<br/><br/>Mar sin anseo is féidir linn a chur ar bun le k arís, agus an fhadhb chéanna a rinne muid roimh., Agus mar sin, is féidir linn a dhéanamh ar an toimhde arís go bhfuil x beag, iarracht a dhéanamh amach é agus a fheiceáil má oibríonn sé. 87 uair 10 go dtí an lúide 4 mhóil - mar sin arís, tá sé ina líon beag. Agus mar sin, casadh sé amach a bheith 1. - níos lú ná 1% de 0.4. Má tá sé níos lú ná 5% den líon níos lú, ní gá duit a bheith buartha faoin líon níos mó, agus mar sin tá do toimhde ceart, ní gá duit an cothromóid cearnach a úsáid.Agus mar sin, ansin is féidir linn a ríomh ag an deireadh go bhfuil an p h anois 3. 31, arís, dhá fhigiúr suntasacha tar éis an ionaid dheachúlach mar gheall ar an toirt.Mar sin, d'athraigh móin 0.1 míle d'aigéad láidir ár p. ó 3 45 go 3 31. Mar sin, d'éirigh linn go maith. Bhí athrú ar p h, chuir muid aigéad agus an p h chuaigh síos, ach ní ag méid ollmhór, mar gheall ar seo iompaigh amach a bheith ina Maolán réasúnta deas anseo, mar sin ní raibh éifeacht an-mhór againn.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá - go bhfuil sampla de fhadhb maolánach.<br/><br/>Mar sin, déanaimis smaoineamh ar nóiméad faoi mhaolán a dhearadh.Breis ort go raibh tú ag iarraidh maolán a chruthú ag p ar leith, cad a chaithfidh tú smaoineamh air? Bhuel, ní mór duit smaoineamh ar an cóimheas idir do aigéad leis an conjungate, agus is gá duit smaoineamh ar an pk a den aigéad, agus an p h sin ag teastáil.<br/><br/>Mar sin tá anseo cothromóid cineálach d’aigéad in uisce, agus mar sin tá ár n-aigéad, h a, i n-ian uisce foirmithe agus inár mbonn comhthiomsaithe. Agus anois táimid ag dul a dhéanamh ar éagsúlacht bheag chun teacht suas le cothromóid a bheadh úsáideach duit i smaoineamh ar Maoláin agus Maoláin a dhearadh.<br/><br/>Mar sin, is féidir linn téarma cineálach a scríobh le haghaidh k. K táirgí cothrom ar ais, agus mar sin tá ár n-ian hidreanium bonn conjugulate os cionn ár n-aigéad., Agus anois is féidir linn a ghlacadh an téarma agus a athshocrú é.Mar sin is féidir linn a tharraingt tiúchan ian hidreaóin ar thaobh amháin. Agus anois táimid ag dul a chur ar an logs den dá thaobh, mar sin a fháil againn an logáil isteach ian hydronium tiúchan, an logáil a, agus an logáil h tiúchan thar lúide tiúchan, agus anois táimid ag dul a iolrú gach rud ag comhartha diúltach, mar sin ní mór dúinn logaí lúide rudaí. Agus tá mé ag dul ach chun é seo a bhogadh anois suas go dtí an barr an scáileáin, agus ní mór dúinn an chothromóid.<br/><br/>Mar sin, cad é logáil lúide tiúchan ian hidrealú?P h. Cad é an logáil lúide k a?, P k a. Mar sin, ní mór dúinn p h is ionann le h ar feadh lúide an logáil tiúchan de do bhonn conjudulate. Agus is tiúchan cothromaíochta iad seo, mar gheall ar an gcuimhne, d'éirigh linn é a fháil ónár léiriú cothromaíochta do k a. Ach go leor uaireanta nuair a bhíonn tú ag obair ar fhadhbanna Maolán, tá a fhios agat na tiúchan a chuir tú leis na rudaí seo, ní gá go mbeadh tiúchan na cothromaíochta.<br/><br/>Agus má athscríobhaimid an abairt seo i dtéarmaí na tiúchana tosaigh nó na tiúchana bunaidh, ansin tá p h thart ar chomh ionann agus pk logáil lúide na dtiúchan tosaigh ar do bhonn comhshúile. Agus tá sé seo ar a dtugtar mar cothromóid Henderson Hasselbach., Agus daoine grá an cothromóid Henderson Hasselbach, agus is féidir é a bheith thar a bheith úsáideach duit chun na fadhbanna seo a réiteach. Ach táim chun béim a leagan nuair atá sé ceart go leor chun é a úsáid agus nuair nach bhfuil sé ceart go leor é a úsáid, mar déanann daoine iarracht é a chur i bhfeidhm ar gach rud, agus tá sé do fhadhbanna maolánacha, agus baineann daoine leis gach cineál rudaí nach fadhbanna maolánacha. Mar sin ba mhaith liom a chinntiú go bhfuil sé soiléir nuair nach féidir leat é a úsáid agus nuair nach féidir leat é a úsáid.Agus tá sé go breá chun é a úsáid agus ba chóir duit é a úsáid nuair atá sé inghlactha, ach ní ag amanna eile.Tá sé ar cheann de na cothromóidí cúpla i mbonn aigéad, mar sin daoine a fháil ar bís ag sé.<br/><br/>Gach ceart, mar sin cuimhnigh go bhfuil sé i ndáiríre cothrom nuair a bhíonn siad ag tiúchan cothromaíochta, agus tá sé seo ach comhfhogasú go bhfuil muid ag rá go bhfuil na tiúchan tosaigh. Mar sin, nuair a bhíonn sé ceart go leor a rá go maith, tá an tiúchan cothromaíochta níos mó nó níos lú mar an gcéanna leis an tiúchan tosaigh. Agus sin fíor nuair a bhíonn x, atá sa sampla seo do tiúchan ian híoróiniam, beag i gcomparáid le do tiúchan tosaigh den aigéad agus an bonn conjulage a chuir tú leis an maolán. Mar sin, agus nuair a bhíonn x beag, ansin tá an tiúchan i bhfad go leor, is ionann an tiúchan cothromaíochta agus an tiúchan tosaigh.Mar sin níl an-athrú i ndáiríre má tá x uimhir i ndáiríre beag.<br/><br/>Mar sin, tá sé seo fíor. Cuimhnigh, táimid ag déanamh maoláin ó aigéid lag agus bunanna conjutag lag, agus is é an sainmhíniú ar aigéad lag go cailleann sé ach codán beag bídeach dá phrótón.Is éard atá ann ná beagán in uisce. Agus is iondúil nach nglacann bonn lag ach codán de na prótón is féidir leis glacadh leis.Is bonn lag é, mar sin níl go leor ceimice á dhéanamh aige ansin.Mar sin tá an t-eolas seo go maith ag an am. Mar sin, ach a fhios agat nuair is féidir leat agus ní féidir é a úsáid, táimid ag dul a thabhairt ar riail agus a rá go bhfuil sé an riail céanna mar a bhí cheana. Nuair a deirimid nuair a bhíonn x beag nó nuair a bhíonn do tiúchan ian hídíniam beag i gcomparáid leis an aigéad nó an bonn conjulate, nuair a bhíonn sé níos lú ná 5%, sin an méid atá á ghlaoch againn beag, mar sin an riail céanna atá á úsáid againn go léir chomh maith. Nuair a bhíonn x beag, oibríonn an comhfhogasú go maith.Agus is féidir leat breiseán a dhéanamh i do thiúis láithreach - do thiúis tosaigh nach bhfuil do thiúchan equirium.<br/><br/>Gach ceart, mar sin a ligean ar úsáid a bhaint as Henderson Hassallch agus dearadh córas maolánach más mian linn a p de 4 6. Agus riail maith a mheabhrú go bhfuil réiteach maolánach is éifeachtaí nuair a tá sé móide nó lúide 1 ar shiúl ó na p a. Mar sin, go bhfuil an rud maith freisin a choinneáil mé i gcuimhne do thaighde chomh maith. Má tá tú i bhfad ar shiúl ó na p k a a, nach bhfuil sé ag dul a bheith ina chóras maolánach maith, agus tá sé seo botún na daoine a dhéanamh sa saotharlann uaireanta, ionas gur féidir leat a reáchtáil isteach sa mhéid sin i roinnt de do chúrsaí saotharlainne.<br/><br/>Mar sin ba mhaith linn maolán faoi p h 4. 6. Mar sin, is féidir linn breathnú ar ár n-srianta ianú d'aigéad, agus ar ndóigh, táimid ag déanamh i gcónaí gach rud ag teocht an tseomra san aonad seo, agus mar sin is féidir linn breathnú ar cad iad na p k a bhfuil roinnt aigéid lag, agus aicéatáit, tá an t-imbhritheoir éasca le do lámha a fháil ar, tá aicéatáit ar fáil go leor, agus tá p maith aige a 4. Mar sin, tá go hiontach, is féidir linn a úsáid go bhfuil an p ceart a dhearadh ár maolánach.Mar sin, tá aigéad aicéiteach an p., agus táimid go léir socraithe, is féidir linn a ullmhú maolán. Ach beidh orainn an t-aigéad agus an t-iondacáit a chur leis, agus ní mór dúinn fios a bheith againn cé mhéad aigéad agus cé mhéad den conjutagrate le cur leis.<br/><br/>Mar sin, is féidir linn a úsáid Henderasselbach cothromóid le haghaidh seo., Táimid ag cruthú maolán, agus mar sin tá sé seo cothrom is féidir linn a úsáid le haghaidh Maoláin. Tá a fhios againn cad p ba mhaith linn, tá a fhios againn ar an p k an rud táimid ag dul a úsáid, agus ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cé mhéad de na aigéad a úsáid agus cé mhéad de na conjulage a úsáid., Ní mór dúinn a fhios ag an cóimheas de amháin go dtí an ceann eile a chur ar bun ár maolán go hidéalach. Mar sin is féidir linn ár n-uimhreacha a phlugáil isteach agus é sin a ríomh.<br/><br/>Mar sin, is féidir linn a athshocrú seo más mian leat ionas go mbeidh an anaithnid ar thaobh amháin agus ní mór dúinn pk a agus an p h ar an taobh eile.Agus mar sin, ní mór dúinn anseo ár p lúide ár p p agus a fháil againn. 15. Anois, ní mór dúinn a logáil isteach agus teacht suas leis an cóimheas, agus an cóimheas gur mhaith linn 1. Mar sin, ba mhaith linn a bheith cóimheas as ár n-aigéad chun an conjugrate de 1 4.<br/><br/>Bhuel cé mhéad go díreach atáimid chun iad a chur leis, tá a fhios againn an cóimheas anois, cé mhéad atáimid ag dul a chur leis? Bhuel, d'fhéadfá a úsáid 1. 4 morálta agus 1 morálta, mar shampla, go mbeadh an cóimheas ceart agat. Ach conas a fhios agat dá mbeadh sé sin go maith? Bhuel, tá an cóimheas níos tábhachtaí ná na méideanna cruinn, áfach, na méideanna a fháil ar an tsaincheist seo de chineál ar an cumas an Maolán - cé chomh resistant go bhfuil sé le hathruithe ar an p h. Mar sin, má úsáideann tú tiúchan ró-íseal den dá cheann, ní bheidh sé sin go léir resistant.Mar sin, más gá duit cumas maolánach níos fearr a bheith agat, na tiúchana níos airde ba chóir duit a úsáid. Agus is féidir leat úsáid a bhaint Hernderassbach ansin a ríomh cén saghas an méid íosta a úsáideann tú, an méid íosta a bhfuil an obair riail 5%.<br/><br/>Mar sin, sa sampla dúirt mé leat faoin riocht seo, bhí an maolán san fhuil go maith, ach bhí an t-aigéad leis an oiread sin go raibh sé ró-mhór ar chumas maolánach. Mar sin dá mbeadh tiúchan níos mó den mhaolán, rud a chuideodh go cinnte sa chás sin. Mar sin, is airde na tiúchana a úsáideann tú, is mó an rud a d'athraigh tú.Agus sin an smaoineamh ar acmhainn maolánacha. Mar sin, agus má úsáideann tú tiúchan ró-íseal, ansin ní bheidh do chothromóid Henderson Hasslbach a bheith bailí fiú.Mar sin is féidir linn dul ar ais agus a ríomh cad é an cineál na tiúchan íosta a chaithfimid a úsáid le haghaidh Henderson Hasslbach a bheith bailí chun freastal ar an riail 5%. Mar sin, le haghaidh p h de 4. 6, is é tiúchan ian híoróine 2. 5 huaire 10 go dtí an lúide 5.<br/><br/>Agus mar sin ansin má oibríonn muid ar gcúl cothrom, ba mhaith linn go mbeadh níos lú ná 5%, agus mar sin tá an tiúchan seo againn thar cheann de na huaireanta sin 100% tugann 100% tú 5%. Mar sin, ní mór don tiúchan a bheith níos mó ná 5 huaire 10 go dtí an lúide 4 móinéir nó ní chomhlíonfaidh tú an riail seo 5%.Mar sin, ar a laghad, a bheith níos mó ná sin agus ní mór dóibh a bheith sa chóimheas cheart. Seachas sin tá tú beagán saor chun cinneadh a dhéanamh cad tá tú ag dul a dhéanamh leis sin, agus d'fhéadfadh go mbeadh breithnithe eile i gceist má tá tú ag obair le tiúchan áirithe de próitéine, ní fhéadfadh tú ag iarraidh go mbeadh do tiúchan maolánacha ró-ard, d'fhéadfadh sé tosú ag cur isteach ar aiste einsím, mar shampla. Agus is minic a mhaoláin áit éigin thart ar 100 milliméir, is é sin tiúchan deas coitianta do mhaoláin.<br/><br/>Gach ceart, mar sin an sleamhnán táimid ag déanamh i ndáiríre go maith., Mar sin labhair muid faoi aigéid lag in uisce, bunanna lag in uisce, agus tá mé ag iarraidh a chur ina luí duit go bhfuil siad mar an gcéanna leis na fadhbanna salainn agus uisce. Mar sin, táimid ag dul chun labhairt i bhfad níos mó faoi salann agus uisce i gceann cúpla nóiméad., Agus dúirt mé leat faoi mhaoláin, ionas go mbeidh muid ach 2 rudaí níos mó le dul. Ní mór dúinn labhairt faoi aigéid láidre in uisce, agus bunanna láidir in uisce, agus ansin beidh gach ceann de na 5 cineálacha fadhbanna a dhéanamh ar an bhfadhb-le-a leagan síos. Agus mar a luaigh mé an uair dheireanach, tá sé decively gearr, tá líon na ceisteanna orthu, ach tá na fadhbanna a tharraingt orthu fada.Mar sin, ná fág iad siúd chuig an nóiméad deireanach nó ní bheidh a lán codlata i gceist. Mar sin, ach focal rabhadh ón am atá caite Breathnaíonn gach duine agus téigh, "Oh, is fadhb-le-chur éasca é seo, níl go leor fadhbanna ann."Mar sin, titration fadhbanna a ghlacadh go leor ama. Mar sin, déanaimis labhairt faoi thorannú, ionas go mbeidh an rang críochnaithe go luath inniu, is féidir leat dul ag obair ar na ceisteanna sin ar an bhfadhb atá leagtha síos.<br/><br/>Gach ceart.Totrials bonn aigéad Mar sin, Cé mhéad agaibh i scoil ard trated aigéad le bonn? codán mór de tú, ceart go leor. Mar sin, de ghnáth cad iad na fadhbanna titration atá i gceist a dhéanamh, go bhfuil rud éigin ar eolas agat faoi aigéad nó bonn, rá an tiúchan aitheanta, agus aigéad tiúchan anaithnid nó meáchan móilíneach anaithnid, agus tú ag bualadh amach, ag bualadh le chéile chun teacht ar fhaisnéis ar iarraidh. Mar sin, is féidir leat tiúchan a chinneadh, uaireanta is féidir leat meáchan móilíneach a chinneadh.<br/><br/>Tá gach ceart, mar sin anseo cad a bhreathnaíonn plota cosúil le agus cuid de na téarmaí tábhachtacha a bhaineann le tréithe bonn aigéad., Mar sin tá tú p h ar thaobh amháin, agus ansin toirt de bonn nó aigéad seo a leanas ar an taobh eile., Mar sin p ţ toirt. Agus an turgnamh iarbhír anseo, tá bonn agat go bhfuil tú ag sileadh, drip amháin ag an am is dócha isteach i do aigéad láidir. Agus ansin tá tú ag iarraidh a fháil amach nuair a bhíonn tú chun teacht ar an ceachtar phointe coibhéis nó an pointe deiridh, is iad seo go bunúsach na téarmaí céanna. Tugtar an pointe stoiciméadrach air freisin, agus is minic a úsáidtear pointe coibhéis mar mhéid teoiriciúil níos mó toirte, áit a gcuirtear an pointe deiridh leis. Mar sin, táimid ag dul a bheith ag caint go leor faoi phointí coibhéise, toisc nach bhfuil aon saotharlainne a bhaineann leis an gcúrsa seo, mar sin beidh gach rud a bheith teoiriciúil i dtéarmaí seo, ach ba chóir iad a bheith mar an gcéanna.<br/><br/>Mar sin, go leor uair a bheidh tú a thomhas p p leis an méadar p h - taispeánann sé seo ach méadar p h. Agus dóibh siúd agaibh a rinne na turgnaimh seo, ba chóir go mbeadh cuma eolach orthu. Agus dóibh siúd agaibh nach bhfuil, go minic cad tá tú ag déanamh nuair a bhíonn tú ag sileadh rud éigin i, tá tú ag fanacht le dath táscaire a athrú mar an pointe deiridh, agus go minic tá tú ag cur leis, soiléir, cad is cosúil a bheith go deo, agus díreach mar a fhaigheann tú thar a bheith impatient, a thosaíonn tú ag cur sé níos tapúla agus a théann tú go léir ar an mbealach chun an dath dorcha. Agus cad ba mhaith leat é seo an-, an-, an-éadrom dath athraithe a léiríonn an deireadh pointe.Mar sin de ghnáth téann tú ró-mhall agus ansin dul ró-tapa agus ansin a dhéanamh ar an turgnamh arís.<br/><br/>D'fhéadfadh iad siúd agaibh a bhfuil aithne acu conas luachanna teoiriciúla a ríomh suí síos agus a dhéanamh ar dtús agus ansin dul i ndáiríre go tapa go dtí go bhfaigheann tú timpeall an phointe seo agus é a chur leis go mall, agus a dhéanamh le saotharlann níos luaithe. Mar sin, beidh muid ag labhairt faoi conas a dhéanann tú an luach teoiriciúil, a d'fhéadfadh a shábháil tú go leor ama má ritheann tú riamh ar fud na saotharlanna in aon rang amach anseo.<br/><br/>Mar sin, seo an dá curves a d’fheicfeá aigéad láidir le bonn láidir, nó bonn láidir le haigéad láidir. Mar sin, má tá tú ag bualadh aigéad láidir le bonn láidir, tá tú chun tús a chur síos a bheith an-aigéad, toisc go bhfuil gach rud a bhfuil tú do aigéad láidir., Agus ansin mar a chuireann tú sa bhonn, beidh an p h dul suas. Feicfidh tú a bheith ag an bpointe seo - tá s do phointe stoic, táimid ag glaoch freisin seo, arís, pointe coibhéis, agus ansin beidh sé ar aghaidh chun dul suas agus ansin tús a chur chun leibhéal suas le beagán níos mó suas anseo.<br/><br/>Má tá tú ag dul ar an treo eile, tá tú ag tosú le bonn láidir, beidh do p h a bheith ard, agus beidh an p h laghdú mar a chuireann tú an t-aigéad., Agus ansin gheobhaidh tú go dtí an pointe stoiciméadrach nó coibhéiseach i lár an chuar anseo. Agus ansin de réir mar a théann tú síos níos faide, níos aigéad, tosaíonn sé ag leibhéal amach.Mar sin, is iad siúd a bhfuil cuma na gcuairí is mó ar an traidisiún.<br/><br/>Mar sin, a ligean ar a dhéanamh ar sampla., Táimid ag dul a bheith acu bonn láidir á titrated le aigéad láidir ar dtús., Agus is é ár n-bunaí láidir n-oigh, agus is é ár n-aigéad láidir h l. Mar sin, a ligean ar ríomh an p h h ag an bpointe coibhéis. Bhuel, a ligean ar ríomh ach an ph ag 5 mils - - Níl a fhios agam an bhfuil sé phointe coibhéis, i ndáiríre. 5 mils seo curtha leis an méid bonn. Mar sin, ní mór dúinn 25 muilte ar ár mbonn láidir ag tiúchan 0.15 moirtéal, agus táimid ag cur 5 mhíle ar ár n-aigéad mólar 0.34.<br/><br/>Mar sin, cad ba mhaith linn a dhéanamh ná an figiúr amach cé mhéad mó mó mó de bonn a bhí againn chun tús a chur leis. Mar sin, cé mhéad móleann de h lúide - arís, is bonn láidir é n o, mar sin féin i bhfad n ó tú a chur leis, is é an méid de h lúide a fhaigheann tú, agus mar sin a chuir muid díreach i líon na n-uaireanta an tiúchan a fháil móilgeallta. Níl aon tábla cothromaíochta ag teastáil uainn anseo.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ansin ba mhaith linn a fháil amach an méid aigéad leis nuair a bhíonn tú ag cur 5 milliúin de sé. Mar sin arís, tá h l aigéad láidir, agus mar sin is ionann an méid h l l agus an méid de h 3 o móide, tá sin déanta, chun críche, agus mar sin chuir muid 5 mils, bhí an tiúchan 0.34 morálta, agus mar sin ní mór dúinn 1 7 uair 10 go dtí an lúide 3 moil.<br/><br/>Mar sin anois, is é an t-aigéad láidir ag dul chun freagairt leis an mbonn láidir, agus ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cé mhéad den bhonn atá fágtha, cé mhéad ian hiodrocsaíde a fhágann tar éis imoibríonn sé leis na hiain hidrigine.Mar sin, imoibríonn sé 1:1, mar sin bhí 6 againn. 25 uair 10 go dtí an lúide 3 mhóil, agus táimid seo a leanas 1 7 huaire 10 go dtí an lúide 3 mhóil an aigéad, agus mar sin táimid ag dul a bheith 4. 55 uair 10 na moil lúide 3 chlé. Agus anois is féidir linn a ríomh ar an moráltacht anseo, mar sin tá líon na mól agus an méid nua againn, agus mar sin bhí 25 mhíle againn sa bhun chun tús a chur leis.Tá 5 mhíle curtha againn leis an aigéad, mar sin is é ár méid nua 30, agus mar sin ní mór dúinn ár tiúchan nua.<br/><br/>Agus anois is féidir linn a ríomh p. An Chéad beidh muid ag ríomh p h, agus ansin a úsáid 14 lúide a fháil ar an p nua h. Mar sin, má bhí tú ag déanamh do cuar tarraingt beag féin, d'fhéadfadh linn a bheith ar an méid aigéad ar thaobh amháin agus p h ar an taobh eile, agus tá tú pointe 13. 18. Sin an méid atá againn tar éis duit 5 muilte seo a leanas., Mar sin ní raibh muid ag thomhas an pointe nialas, ach tá a fhios againn cad é pointe amháin tar éis 5 mils an aigéad curtha leis.<br/><br/>Mar sin anois, a ligean ar dul ceart go dtí an pointe coibhéis - - mar sin ar an mbealach a tharraingt mé ba chóir a chur in iúl nach raibh pointe coibhéis., Mar sin, ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cé mhéad is gá dúinn a chur leis an aigéad chun teacht ar an pointe coibhéis. Mar sin ciallaíonn an pointe coibhéis nó an pointe stoiciméadrach go gcuireann tú an méid céanna móil de do ghteatán agus a bhí agat, agus mar sin tá siad ag dul a bheith comhionann lena chéile. Mar sin, má tá tú ag cur aigéad go bonn láidir, tá tú seo a leanas go leor aigéad go bhfuil tú anois ar líon comhionann na móil ar an mbonn go raibh tú chun tús a chur leis., Mar sin tá a fhios againn 6. 25 uair 10 go dtí an lúide 3 mhóil an bonn i láthair.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ag an bpointe coibhéise, ní mór duit a bheith go bhfuil an líon céanna mó móil aigéad., Mar sin, tá a fhios againn an tiúchan an aigéid atá againn agus an líon móilín ní mór dúinn, agus mar sin is féidir linn a ríomh ar an toirt, atá 18. 4 milliméadar, nó 0.0184 lítear. Mar sin, cad a bheadh an p h ansin a bheith ar an bpointe coibhéis?, Táimid ag titating aigéad láidir le bonn láidir, cad ba chóir an p h a bheith 7. Mar sin áit éigin timpeall anseo táimid ag dul a bheith ag 18, ionas gur féidir leat iarracht a dhéanamh cuar a tharraingt, agus anseo tá p 7 nuair a tá muid imithe go dtí thart 18. 4 milliméadar seo a leanas.<br/><br/>Mar sin, nuair a dhéanann tú aitreall aigéad láidir le bonn láidir, foirm tú salann go bhfuil neodrach.Mar gheall ar an conjugates d'aigéad láidir agus nach bhfuil bonn láidir bunúsach nó aigéadach., Tá siad neamhéifeachtach mar aigéid nó bunanna, mar sin tá tú ag dul a fháil salann neodrach. Mar sin, má tá tú ag déanamh fadhb le aigéad láidir agus bonn láidir, nach bhfuil sé a lán de na ríomhanna gur gá a dhéanamh ag an bpointe seo, is gá duit ach a aithint gur chóir do salann a bheith neodrach.<br/><br/>Mar sin anois, is féidir linn labhairt faoi cad a tharlaíonn má chuireann tú milliméadar breise de do aigéad, tar éis duit an pointe coibhéis sin a bhaint amach, rud is dócha a rinne daoine gan na tréithe seo a dhéanamh, imithe i bhfad níos faide ná an pointe coibhéise.<br/><br/>Mar sin, an chéad is féidir linn a fháil amach ar líon na móil den aigéad láidir go bhfuil muid seo a leanas breise, mar sin a dúirt muid ar cheann milf ar an aigéad láidir, agus mar sin arís, téann sé a chríochnú. An tiúchan, is é an aigéad 0.34 amanna morálta ár 1 míle, mar sin tá muid a leanas 3. 4 huaire 10 go dtí an lúide 4 móil bhreise ar ár aigéad, agus mar sin a ligean ar ríomh ansin cad é an moráltacht go bhfuil muid a chur leis go raibh breise.<br/><br/>Agus fiú más rud é nach bhfuil tú ag áireamhán, tá muid chabhraigh tú amach. A ligean ar a ghlacadh ach 10 soicind níos mó.<br/><br/>Sílim gur é seo an chéad cheann, nach bhfuil,? An raibh mé a lua tá sé smaoineamh maith chun tús a chur leis an bhfadhb-le-a leagtar go luath., Mar sin, bhí an cleas anseo mar gheall ar an toirt., Mar sin, bhí 25 míle againn chun tús a chur leis, ansin chuir muid 18. 4 a fháil chun an pointe coibhéis, agus ansin tá muid 1 míle thar an bpointe coibhéise., Agus na cleasanna chun na codanna de na fadhbanna a bhfuil a mheabhrú ar fad na rudaí a cuireadh leis a fháil ar an toirt.<br/><br/>Mar sin, nach bhfuil an fhadhb féin an-bhlasta go bhfuil ach aigéad in uisce, ach tá an cleas amháin i ríomh an moráltacht, ag cuimhneamh ar gach rud a cuireadh leis a fháil chun an bpointe seo.
