Níl an cogadh trámizing do gach duine, nach bhfuil luí seoil trámagasc do gach duine, ach tá an dá foinsí tábhachtacha PTSD le haghaidh roinnt.<br/><br/>I ndáiríre, an níos mó a Sílim faoi, is é an rud is frustrachasaí atá orm nach bhfuil an comhrá seo ag dul i ngleic ar rud éigin mar sin gan phointe agus nach mbaineann. An ndearna an tuairisc a roghnaíodh is dócha a dhéanamh ar an comparáid le cogadh (seachas, a rá, "Is féidir le luí seoil a bheith mar matamating mar timpistí gluaisteán") toisc go bhfuil sé níos mó pearsanta agus is dócha aird a tharraingt?, Cinnte, is rud a tharlaíonn. Tarlaíonn sé freisin gur dóichí go smaoineoidh daoine dáiríre ar thionchar tráma iarphróidiam toisc go gceapann daoine ar fad go bhfuil an cogadh níos tromchúisí agus níos treacha ná eachtraí "gach lá" cosúil le timpistí gluaisteán agus breith linbh, fiú má tá siad mícheart. (Séatar go staitistiúil, is coitianta eachtra trámamach a d’fhéadfadh a bheith ann, is é an cion is mó de chásanna PTSD a bheidh ann ná a bheith cuntasach! Anuraidh fuair mé taithí ar chóireáil thuisceanach le roinnt dochtúirí agus altraí le linn mo thoircheas agus seachadadh le déanaí. Bhí na heispéiris seo ar fad ar siúl in ospidéal mór Ollscoile i gcathair mhór, is cuma cén luach sin.<br/><br/>Níl mé ag caitheamh amach ach cáineadh treallacha chun an ghairm leighis a mharú.Tá mé ag labhairt as an gcéad taithí láimhe.B'fhéidir go dtugann sé mo thuairim freisin go pointe áirithe a d'úsáid mé a bheith ag obair mar aturnae cosanta leighis. Chonaic mé cuid de na cásanna is measa a bhí á n-imirt i mo shaol féin agus i saol eile. Níl dochtúirí, altraí agus paraimhíochaineoirí eile ach daonna, is féidir leo agus botúin a dhéanamh, agus tugann siad go leor dá dtuairimí pearsanta féin agus claontachtaí don tábla mar aon leo.<br/><br/>Mar sin, ba mhaith liom a iarraidh ort nach bhfuil le do thoil glacadh go bhfuil mé aineolach nó spreagtha ag an bhfear., Is é mo fráma tagartha ach difriúil ó mise, deireadh an scéil.<br/><br/>tá an córas a oibríonn tú le haghaidh briste. Ní dhéanann duine amháin nach bhfuil ina dhuine uafásach ach coinníollacha a fheabhsú do na mná go léir, go háirithe nuair a bhíonn altraí agus dochtúirí eile fós ag an uimhir a fheiceann tú nach bhfuil ag déanamh a ndícheall chun gan mná a mhealladh. Is é an t-aon bhealach atá sa chóras ná feabhas a chur ar mhná labhairt faoin tráma a chuir ospidéil tríd iad, agus roghanna eile a lorg.<br/><br/>Go raibh maith agat.Tored de na dochtúirí agus na altraí a fheiceáil a thaispeántar mar rang sna tuairimí.Tá sé chomh dian sin nach dtugann mé faoi deara é níos mó fós.Ach tá sé ann, cion bunaithe ar stádas a ghealladh trí bheith i do bhall den ghairm leighis.<br/><br/>Má tá leanbh ina leanbh is féidir a bheith chomh strus mar timpiste carr mór, NÍL cogadh.<br/><br/>Níl mé ag iarraidh mná a bhfuil PTSD acu a íoslaghdú mar gheall ar imeachtaí le linn nó a gcuid breithe-is stoirm iontach é an rud is cúis le PTSD. Tá tráma fisiciúil gan choinne, a dhéantar le do chorp, ag daoine eile an cur síos is mó ar chás PTSDAch ní féidir liom a rá go bhfuil breith mar an gcéanna le bheith ina sibhialta nó fiú saighdiúir i gcríoch naimhdeach le seachtainí, míonna nó blianta, tá sé díreach ní hamháin.<br/><br/>Bhuel is rud maith ansin nach bhfuil aon duine ag déanamh maíomh blaincéad i ndáiríre go bhfuil breith i gcónaí mar trám agus dá bhrí sin cosúil le heispéiris snámha.<br/><br/>Tá tuiscint ann go mbíonn comharthaí strus iar-threacha ann mar thoradh ar imeacht atá neamhghnách nó lasmuigh de ghnáth-thaithí an duine," a dúirt Deborah Da Costa, síceolaí McGill a chomh-údaraigh an staidéar.<br/><br/>Ach tá rudaí is féidir a tharlóidh sa phróiseas breithe a dhéanamh go mbraitheann bean cosúil lena saol nó a saol leanbh i gcontúirt.Tá sí i bhfad níos lú Gan amhras, eagla, horror. sin go leor le haghaidh taithí a bheith trámach."<br/><br/>Tá mé ag dul amach ar leamh anseo agus ag oscailt go bhfuil othair breithe a dhéanann aghaidh i ndáiríre ar an saol bagairt cúinsí iad féin agus / nó dá leanaí níos mó seans chun taithí a fháil helplessness, eagla agus horror, agus is ea, tráma iarbhír neamh-samhladúil mar thoradh ar na cúinsí sin. Mar sin, cad, deirimid leo nach bhfuil siad riamh os comhair horror ar scála na ndaoine atá ag fulaingt ar dtús go bhfuil siad i dteideal a bheith trámatha nó taithí a fháil ar PTSD?<br/><br/>Aontaím le Tinfoil Hattiese thuas, is é an t-aon chúis go ndéantar an analaí cogaidh mar gheall go bhfuil muid anseo sna Stáit Aontaithe go ginearálta eolach air i gcomhthéacs na saighdiúirí tar éis dóibh filleadh ó chogadh. Tá sé ar an drochuair, mar is cosúil go bhfuil an plé ag dul a dhíscríobh i gcineál opolitics onpression a bhfuil sé níos measa. Mar gheall ar ndóigh, is iad sibhialtaigh agus saighdiúirí (is é sin, fir) i gcúinsí te, an t-aon duine a d'fhéadfaí a dhíchóimeáil go fírinneach ar an leibhéal taithí a fháil ar PTSD. Ní bunch den chéad domhan, cén fáth go ndearna mná gearán faoi conas a dtaithí ar bhreith iad a scríobadh ar feadh an tsaoil.<br/><br/>Nach bhfreastalaíonn ansin ach tacaíocht bhreise a sholáthar do chultúr an tost a chuireann cosc ar mhná aitheantas a thabhairt dá gcuid riachtanas nó cóireáil a fháil air.<br/><br/>Eh. Sílim go mbraitheann sé ar an bhreith.Is dócha go mbeidh bean raped a thugann breith i gcrios cogaidh le deacrachtaí tromchúiseacha traumatized go leor. Tá bean a bhfuil hemorrhage postpartum a bhfuil an dochtúir bata a chuid/a dhorn isteach sa uterus w/o drugaí, ansin tá stillbirth chun tosaithe, ag dul a bheith suas go bhfuil go maith.<br/><br/>Is é mo phointe, tá sé beagán amaideach chun tús a chur leis an ngnó "a leithéid agus den sórt sin a chatagóir tráma níos measa ná a leithéid agus den sórt sin a tráma catagóir."Braitheann sé ar an staid agus na daoine lena mbaineann. Go háirithe nuair a bhíonn cuid den tráma cogaidh do mhná sibhialta chomh ceangailte le foréigean gnéasach, breith, agus leanaí.<br/><br/>Sea, is é an rud le PTSD ná nach bhfuil a symptom rangú * féin i dtéarmaí aon ordlathas mar sin - is é an taithí, cé go bhfuil sé i gcomhthéacs sóisialta, fós ceann pearsanta. Is féidir le duine a bhfuil PTSD aige ó timpiste gluaisteán a bheith ag fulaingt ach an oiread agus a bhaineann le duine le PTSD ó bheith i gcogadh. Ifreann, d'fhéadfadh duine a bhí * i gcogadh * a bheith ina PTSD fós ó timpiste gluaisteán! Agus d'fhéadfadh a bheith comharthaí níos measa fós ó na dara ceann ag brath ar an taithí a tharla i ndáiríre agus cad a chiallaigh sé dóibh. Mar shampla, tá torthaí ann gur gnách go mbíonn riosca níos mó ag paisinéirí*car * paisinéirí* orthu ná tiománaí carr ** chun PTSD a fhorbairt tar éis timpiste, is é an teoiric é sin mar phaisinéir níos lú smachta agat ar an méid a tharla agus dá bhrí sin tá sé níos deacra déileáil leis an méid a tharla agus a réiteach. Tá mothúcháin a bhaineann le cailliúint rialaithe coitianta do thimpistí carr, breith linbh, cogadh, agus go leor eachtraí a d'fhéadfadh a bheith tubaisteach eile, ach go teoiriciúil d'fhéadfadh duine cogadh a fhulaingt faoi chúinsí a mheasann siad níos mó i gceannas ná mar a bheadh orthu i dtimpiste ghluaisteáin nó i dtimpiste ghluaisteáin, ionas go mbeidh cogadh níos neamhghnách ná mar is gá breith a thabhairt nó a bheith i dtimpiste ghluaisteáin. (Cé go mbraitheann daoine go hiomlán go bhfuil aon smacht acu, mar sin tá mé díreach tar éis hipitéis faoi conas nach bhfuil brí sóisialta an taithí trámaigh ábhartha i gcónaí mar bhrí phearsanta an é.)<br/><br/>Ní leanann tráma síceolaíoch rialacha sóisialta – baineann sé le tuiscint phearsanta ar imeacht, ní hamháin a bhrí shóisialta níos mó (cé gur chóir a thabhairt faoi deara freisin gur féidir le strus sóisialta cosúil le easpa airgeadais, fadhbanna sláinte nó deacrachtaí eile acmhainní a bhaineann le cóipeáil a scriosadh go hiomlán agus so-ghabhálach i leith PTSD a mhéadú ar an mbealach sin). Mar sin, níl aon phointe ar bith ag gach rud a rangú bunaithe ar an gcaoi ar cheart dóibh * a bheith ag déanamh trádaireachta, braitheann sé ar an duine a bhfuil taithí aige ar an imeacht agus ar an méid a chiallaíonn sé dóibh, d’fhéadfadh go mbeadh difríocht mhór ann idir PTSD a fuarthas thar thréimhse fhada ama agus PTSD a fuarthas ó eachtra trámach amháin. Mar sin féin ní fheicim conas atá ábhartha fiú.<br/><br/>Sea, is é an rud le PTSD ná nach bhfuil a symptom rangú * féin* i dtéarmaí aon ordlathas mar sin<br/><br/>Ní chuireann sé ciall dom iarracht a dhéanamh é sin a rangú; ní gá go mbeadh déine na n-airíonna sách soiléir agus go leor ag baint le déine measta na tráma bunúsach.Ní dhéanann sé ach méid a imirt ar Cluichí Oilimpeacha Freasúra.<br/><br/>Ní raibh mé riamh ina gcónaí i gcrios cogaidh, agus, le mo aiféala síoraí (nach mhaith liom a rá riamh mar sin le mo mhac, ar ndóigh), ní raibh an deis agam breith a thabhairt don leanbh agus ní bheidh riamh.<br/><br/>Ach tá mé imithe trí mo sciar cothrom de cad a Creidim gur féidir a mheas "tuama dlisteanach" (chun bonn a chur ar frása Akinsque), gach ceann acu difear go láidir dom, agus amhras mé go bhfuil mé fuair riamh go hiomlán thar aon cheann de. Ach cibé an gcáilíonn mo imoibriú maidir le haon cheann de (gearrthéarma nó fadtéarmach) go teicniúil mar PTSD nó nach bhfuil (ní bheadh a fhios agam, ós rud é nach bhfuil mé i gcomhairle le duine ar bith faoin gceist áirithe sin), is cinnte nach dóigh liom go bhfuil déine na n-éifeachtaí tar éis aon phatrún ar leith.<br/><br/>Féach, díreach ag fás suas mar a bhí leanbh marthanóir Uilelosaimh ag déanamh rudaigh dom ar go leor bealaí. Mar sin bhí fear a bhí de bharr soicind níos luaithe (ach a raibh a súile fós oscailte agus is cosúil ag féachaint ar dom), ina luí ar cosa cúpla ar shiúl ó dom ar an taobh eile den doras ar ais go dtí ár árasán, nuair a bhí mé 9 bliain d'aois agus d'oscail an doras mar gheall ar chuala mé fuaim casachta os ard. Mar sin bhí mé mí-úsáid arís agus arís eile ag dochtúir i mo dhéagóir luatha. Mar sin bhí mé i mo phaisinéir i gcarr ag tiomáint mo mháthair nuair a fuair sí i dtimpiste ar an Tras-Bronx Expressway mar thoradh ar a bás, nuair a bhí mé 20. (Ní féidir liom breathnú go fóill, agus uaireanta a fháil flashbacks, nuair a théann mé timpiste agus iad ag tiomáint nó níl timpiste carr i scannán nó ar an teilifís.) Mar sin tá tinneas ainsealach incurable ag fulaingt le 30+ bliain anuas, iomlán le gach cineál pian uafásach agus máinliacht Uafásach agus deacrachtaí máinliachta Uafásach agus éagsúlacht na n-eispéiris gar-Báise. Mar sin, bhí an tar éis mo chuid GRS, ach codán díobh a labhair mé anseo, nó a mbeidh sé riamh, Tá súil agam go dtuigeann fiú iad siúd agaibh cé chomh buan agus atá sé trámagáil a bheith ag fás suas sa saol seo.<br/><br/>Agus mar sin de.<br/><br/>Ach ní féidir liom a rá go hionraic go raibh aon cheann de sin níos mó ná traumatizing dom cad a bhí sé cosúil le mo ex a chuaigh faoi éigeandáil C-Alt tar éis a chonaic mé ár mac's chroí dul síos go dtí beagnach aon rud ar an monatóireacht marfach, mar gheall ar tharla sé, bhí fillte an téad umbilical thart ar a mhuineál. Ach an gníomh a scríobh a dhéanann síos mo pulse tús a rásaíocht, go léir na blianta ina dhiaidh sin, níl mé le fios go raibh an méid a bhraith mé mar an gcéanna le cad a chuaigh mo ex trí., Ní raibh mé a thabhairt breithe dó Ach bhí sé mo leanbh freisin.<br/><br/>Ní dhéanfainn iarracht na rudaí seo go léir a rangú (Céim ar dheis, éisteacht leis na rudaí seo go léir faoi, an 10ú Tráma is fearr i Donna!), agus ná bí cinnte go bhfuil sé de cheart é sin a dhéanamh.<br/><br/>OK, mar sin go breá, níl aon phointe ann PIST a phlé tar éis an phas-partum.Tosaíonn Jill agus údar an ailt tríd an bhfocal a úsáid strus, agus ansin is cosúil go n-éireoidh sé an focal struis leis an bhfocal a chur i gcoinne an stramáil focal. Ansin, rinne siad an bonn a thuilleadh de chás chun an t-aláram os cionn PPPND a shlánú, trí bhreith linbh a chur i gcomparáid le cogadh.<br/><br/>Buille faoi thuairim mé go bhfuil mar gheall ar suimeanna suas.<br/><br/>Agus anois beidh mé a fháil ar a dtugtar ainmneacha, arís, agus beidh an scuad lámhaigh ciorclach ar aghaidh agus ar aghaidh go dtí an pointe bunaidh an bpost Jill agus beidh an t-alt nasctha sí a bheith caillte go hiomlán.<br/><br/>Níl. D’fhéadfadh sé a bheith cosúil ar an gcéad amharc go bhfuil sé mar gheall ar bhreith trámach a chothromú go heispéiris chogaidh mar iarracht úlla i gcomparáid, ach conas is féidir a fhios ag duine ón taobh amuigh i ndáiríre go bhfuil a leithéid le cinnteacht go hiomlán. An bhfuil sé i ndáiríre go deacair a chur féin i bróga eile agus a thuiscint go raibh a dtaithí pearsanta fíor, uafásach traumatizing?<br/><br/>Tarlaíonn sé le spreagthóirí freisin. Forbraíonn daoine áirithe triggers i ndáiríre gan choinne agus idé-ocsainciúla (cosúil le táibléid dearg nó boladh na hadaimh) as a dturama mar gheall ar nádúr comhghaolmhar an inchinn – aeroiriúnaithe agus gach a. Ach tá mé le feiceáil go leor de na daoine aineolach na triggers toisc nach bhfuil siad a dhéanamh "nó a chiall" nó " nach bhfuil tromchúiseach" (Tabhair faoi deara: nach bhfuil siad seo na cineálacha de triggers is féidir a rabhadh le haghaidh go hiontaofa mar sin a ligean ar a fháil isteach sa deireabhsú, má bhí aon duine sin claonta.) Tá an inchinn an duine aisteach.Ní oibríonn sé ar bhealaí is féidir a rialú nó a chatagóiriú go héasca.Ní thuigimid go hiomlán cén fáth a fhorbraíonn daoine PTSD uaireanta agus nach mbíonn siad uaireanta eile, fiú i ndáiríre i gcásanna tromchúiseacha. Mar shampla, bíonn tubaistí nádúrtha i gceist * gan an oiread PTSD a tháirgeadh, sílim go bhfuil an teoiric a d’fhéadfadh a bheith ag daoine cé nach féidir leo na himeachtaí seo a rialú, go bhfuil siad ag fulaingt ar a laghad le chéile agus gur féidir leo teacht le chéile chun tacú lena chéile, agus tá míniú ar an méid a tharla agus cén fáth. Ach tá eisceachtaí ann freisin – is cosúil gur tharla go leor PTSD, cé nach bhfuil an oiread sin ann don hairicín féin ach an tar éis, nach raibh a lán tubaiste "nádúrtha" ann. (Tá mo fhoinse ar seo caite má tá sé níos mó meánbhunaithe ná taighde-bhunaithe, áfach – oscailte do cheartú.) Tá an inchinn an duine aisteach.Ní oibríonn sé ar bhealaí is féidir a rialú nó a chatagóiriú go héasca.<br/><br/>Tá an oiread sin fadhbanna ann nach féidir an rud seo a shárú.<br/><br/>Ach tá eisceachtaí ann freisin – is cosúil gur tharla go leor PTSD, cé nach bhfuil an oiread sin ann don hairicín féin ach an tar éis, nach raibh a lán tubaiste "nádúrtha" ann.<br/><br/>Chun cad tá sé fiú, tá comhlacht mór go leor taighde a thugann le fios go bhfuil PTSD cosúil go háirithe le tarlú nuair a bhíonn an t-iompar trámach ceangailte le ghnáthamh laethúil - is é sin, tiomáint carr, béile, siúl ag foirgneamh áirithe ar do bhealach abhaile, is cuma cad. Is féidir le rudaí a tharlaíonn i gcásanna go hiomlán lasmuigh de raon feidhme an ghnáth-thíorthúil sin PTSD (ar ndóigh) a chur faoi deara fós ach is lú an seans go ndéanfaidh siad amhlaidh. Sampla ná gur lú an seans go bhforbródh daoine atá ar mhúcailt agus iad ag taisteal i dtíortha eile ná daoine atá i mbéal an phobail féin.<br/><br/>Tráma idirphearsanta, is é sin, imeachtaí trámacha de bharr an duine i dtreo daoine eile a shíl de ghnáth go bhfuil níos mó tráma neamh-idirphearsanta, ie tubaistí nádúrtha. Ní hé sin le rá nach féidir le daoine a bheith tar éis imeacht gan choinne, ach go bhfuil na rátaí difriúil. Is é roinnt smaointe ar cén fáth é seo, go bhfuil a betrayal nuair a hurts duine amháin eile a excarates an trámach-muil (beidh mé a dhéanamh suas focail más mian liom!) ar an ócáid. Tá sé cosúil le bleach a rá chomh nimhiúil le taos fiacla, mar má itheann tú 1000 feadáin de taos fiacla, is dócha go n-éireodh leat, mar a bheadh cupán amháin de tuaradh.<br/><br/>Tá an oiread sin ró-ionadaíocht ag na rialtais i ráitis meabhairshláinte nuair a chuireann tú gach tráma san áireamh (féinmharaithe, strus srl) ní chuirfeadh sé iontas orm dá mba iad na huimhreacha is mó.<br/><br/>Bhuel, ar a laghad, is cosúil go bhfuil an chuid trácht den chuid is mó a laghdú ina pléascadh thar an téarmaíocht a chur in iúl gur é an téarmaíocht an t-aon rud a figured dúinn pointe ar an ábhar. Sílim gur féidir linn go léir a aontú go bhfuil baint ag an bpáirtí le PTSD le hábhar nár tugadh aird níos mó air ná mar a fhaigheann sé.<br/><br/>Ag léamh na tuairimí go léir, bhí mé i ndáiríre ag súil le duine amháin ar a laghad a chimed le díshealbhú "ach tá mná ag déanamh seo ar feadh na mílte bliain, mar sin ná bí chomh áibhéile.", Go bhfaigheann de ghnáth amach ann ag pointe éigin. Mar sin, b'fhéidir sa snáithe seo go bhfuil sé i ndáiríre an téarmaíocht go bhfuil daoine greamaithe ar.Is dearfach é sin, buille faoi thuairim mé., Íocann sé go leor i bhfad suas dom chomh maith, Jadey.<br/><br/>Cibé an bhfuil sé an gnéasas inmheánach ar ár sochaí, nó an brú i gcoinne "máthair domhain" reitric, I mo thuairimse, tá claonadh i dtreo a bhriseadh luí seoil agus an taithí is féidir a tharlóidh ar an mbealach seo ar ais agus amach thar cogaidh! nach bhfuil cosúil le luí seoil! plé.<br/><br/>Agus téann mé ar ais go dtí mo phointe níos luaithe fós, cé go bhféadfadh duine smaoineamh ar cé chomh trámach a bhí fiú do dhuine eile, níl aon bhealach ann fós dearbhú den sórt sin a dhéanamh go hionraic. Toisc go bhfuil daoine go léir difriúil, agus nach n-oibríonn a n-inchinn go léir ar an mbealach céanna nó imeachtaí saoil a phróiseáil ar an mbealach céanna. Toisc go bhfuil sé sexism, le taobh olc de misgyny thrown i.<br/><br/>Tá mé faoi chóireáil le haghaidh PTSD ar agus amach ar feadh cúpla scór bliain. Bhí mo chéad bhreith an-tearcanta, agus chomhdhéantaigh sé na hairíonna PTSD a bhí agam cheana féin as an locht ar ionsaí gnéasach. Bhí mo toirchis is déanaí mar chuid de shraith tubaiste (leanbh agus tá mé breá! gach rud a mheas) a raibh c-alt bhaiscithe i gcás ina damáiste néaróg pelvic agus an cealú mo pháirtí. Ní féidir liom labhairt leis na staitisticí atá nasctha thuas, ach is é mo thaithí phearsanta ná má tá PTSD agat cheana féin, gur tréimhse an-leochaileach an toircheas agus an luí seoil ina bhfuil mná PTSD. Mothaíonn do chorp as do smacht, tá daoine ag dreapadh na ballaí chun a rá leat cad atá le déanamh, conas a bheith, agus cé tú féin, agus go bhfuil tú níos mó ag brath ar dhaoine eile ná mar atá tú de ghnáth. Tá sé freisin ar coaster sorcóir mhothúchánach, ach mar sin thar a bheith mór., Níl sé ina stráice deacair dom a fheiceáil conas a d'fhéadfadh bean comhdhéanta ar shlí eile teacht amach ar an taobh eile de toirchis a bhfuil tráma suntasach.<br/><br/>Go raibh maith agat as a roinnt, Aon Is dóigh liom gurbh é seo an plé a theastaigh uaim a fheiceáil. Sular léigh mé é seo, níor cheap mé i ndáiríre faoin éifeacht atá ann cheana PTSD, ionsaí gnéasach, nó tráma a bheadh ar an taithí a bhí ag breith. Tá sé ciallmhar go hiomlán go ndéanfadh PTSD a bhí ann cheana féin duine thar a bheith leochaileach le linn toirchis, luí seoil agus taithí tuismitheoir. Tá tú chomh ceart go bhfuil daoine cosúil go mbraitheann siad cosúil le tá siad anois an ceart a insint duit cad a dhéanamh agus conas é a dhéanamh.<br/><br/>Maidir liom féin, ba chúis le mo chéad toirchis dom go mór le dúlagar (partum dúlagar, b'fhéidir).Ní dóigh liom fiú gur thuig mé cé chomh suarach agus a bhí mé ag mothú go dtí go raibh mé ag mothú níos fearr ar deireadh (nuair a bhí an toirchis os a chionn). Bhí an toirchis ach aon deacracht uafásach agus tubaiste tar éis a chéile agus bhí mé ar leaba beagnach an t-am ar fad., Roimhe sin, bhí mé ag aturnae cleachtadh sa chathair agus bhí mé i ndáiríre gníomhach go fisiciúil. Ní raibh an toirchis pleanáilte (ní gá, in aghaidh an fheiceáil, ach beidh mé ag rá ach nach raibh sé ag an am go maith) agus tá a fhios agam go bhfuil a chuidigh. Le linn an toirchis dúradh liom a bheith "ádh" dá n-áirigh mé é an marc 30 seachtain agus go mbeadh sé beagnach dodhéanta dul thar 35 woks. Bhí mé chomh buartha go raibh mé ag dul tríd seo go léir ach a chailleadh an leanbh nó leanbh a bheith agam san NICU bhí ag streachailt le maireachtáil (a bhfuil, a fhios agam, a tharlaíonn do go leor daoine). Cad a rinne sé i bhfad níos deacra go bhfuil na daoine a choinneáil ag rá, "Tá tú an t-ádh a bheith ag iompar clainne fiú "Is é seo an toirchis a miracle. "Níl ach buíochas le Dia do gach lá". Chuir sé orm gur mhaith liom nach raibh aon cheart agam mothú depressed agus trína chéile. Níor bhraith sé bronntanas. Mhothaigh sé cosúil le pionós. Rinne mé mo thuairimse, mar a bhí mé i mo mháthair dona a bhraitheann fiú salach mar gheall ar an toirchis agus buartha faoi mo shláinte féin, chomh maith le a bhfuil leanbh sláintiúil agam., Rinne mé é go dtí 37 wks agus bhí mé i pian an oiread sin go leor i bhfad ar an am ar fad. Bhí orm go raibh incision ingearach C-alt/meabhrán (tá an scarring beagnach sé orlach ar fad) agus fuair mé dhá mhála fola.Bhí an gearradh inmheánach ar mo uterus den sórt sin nach bhféadfainn súil a bheith agam go mbeadh VAC agat riamh. Bhí sé ceart go leor sa deireadh, ach bhí an toirchis díreach uafásach agus, tá, I mo thuairimse, 'traismeach' bheadh an focal ceart.<br/><br/>Sílim go bhfuil cuid den fáth a bhfuil sé chomh deacair labhairt faoi seo toisc go bhfuil i gcónaí scéal níos measa amach ann agus cuirtear in iúl do mhná a bheith i gcónaí TANKFUL nach raibh sé níos measa. Go raibh maith agat go raibh muid ag iompar clainne, ár saol ár saol, buíochas a bhí againn tar éis leanaí... srl, an fhírinne é, is féidir linn a bheith buíoch as na rudaí sin go léir agus ag an am céanna a aithint cé chomh deacair agus is féidir é a bheith agus cé chomh deacair is féidir, do mhná go leor. Cén fáth nach féidir le toircheas a bheith 'nádúrtha' agus 'álainn' do roinnt daoine eile agus atá ag dul in olcas?Tá muid go léir difriúil agus taithí a fháil ar rudaí éagsúla. Nuair a bhíonn ár dtaithí tostaithe ag ráitis mar, "go maith, tá an t-ádh ort, tá a fhios agam duine éigin a choinníonn sé i ndáiríre ó bheith ag leighis ó ár scéalta agus ár bhfeiceann nach bhfuil muid ina n-aonar.<br/><br/>Go díreach. Go raibh maith agat!Bhí mo chéad ionsaí gnéasach uafásach. Bhí mo chéad toirchis agus mo bhreith uafásach, ach ar bhealach eile, bhí mo dara toircheas uamhnach agus bhí an bhreith ach go breá.Cén fáth go bhfuil daoine crochadh suas ar na ráitis ridiculous seo a dhéanamh?<br/><br/>An mbeadh sé a dhéanamh aon difríocht do na marthanóirí anseo má bhí an t-alt headráin, "Is féidir breith a bheith chomh struis mar éigniú"?<br/><br/>Tá sé seo fuair a bheith ar an iarracht is mó ag gotta mé b'fhéidir le feiceáil riamh anseo ar Femministe.<br/><br/>Rud uafásach a d’fhulaing mná a d’fhéadfadh a bheith ina gcúis le tráma mothúchánach a athrú.<br/><br/>Childbirth? Rud na mban (chomh maith le tras fir) a bhfuil an cumas chun dul go dona mícheart agus faoi deara athrú tráma mhothúchánach.<br/><br/>Tá an iarracht arís agus arís eile ag cúpla trolls anseo chun a mbealach a dhéanamh chun tosaíocht a thabhairt do mhná nó a n-fhulaingt nó tá fulaingt daoine eile ag éirí leo agus ag maslach. Tagann rud éigin cliste agus tuisceanach le cur leis an bplé nó le cailliúint cheana féin.<br/><br/>Go deimhin, is é ceann de na rudaí nach féidir linn a "rialú", is cuma cé mhéad a chuireann na pleananna breithe agus na leabhair agus na scéalta idirlín atá ag daoine eile in iúl dúinn.Is féidir é a dul sásta ag aon dara faoi leith |
| Law and "Right and Wrong"<br/><br/>Comments over my last post show just how personal perceptions can color views of matters, especially of law. So… let's talk about law. In general terms, criminal law is the codification of those offenses which society has termed unacceptable and which the governing authority has codified and promulgated, defining the offense and prescribing the penalties or range of penalties for violating that law. Law is not an "ideal"; it is a code. Nor does that code always agree with what many citizens feel it should be, or even what many people thinks its provisions mean. That's one reason for courts, and why the U.S. Supreme Court has the power to review laws passed by Congress, or by state and local governments.<br/><br/>Initially, copyright infringement was strictly a matter of civil law, but copyright violations became so egregious in the late nineteenth century that in 1897 Congress enacted the first very narrow criminal penalties for copyright infringement, but in less than a decade it became clear that a more comprehensive approach was required, and the first broad law criminalizing a range of copyright infringements was enacted in 1909. Since then, various provisions have been added to criminal copyright law, including the No Electronic Theft (NET) act in 1997, and the Digital Millennium Copyright Act of 1998.<br/><br/>As is noted by most good property law scholars, "property" is not legally limited to tangible objects, but much of the controversy over copyright infringement or theft of intellectual property lies in the fact that the vast majority of people do not place the same emotional value on the copying of an ebook or a music download as they would on someone taking a physical copy of the same book or a music CD from a store without paying for it. Yet current law places the same value on theft of a digital book or an authorized digital copy of a song as of a physical book or music CD. And it should, because for the bookstore, the wholesaler, and the author, that "free" copy represents a very real loss. It is a foregone sale, just as the theft of a physical book or CD represents a foregone sale. Admittedly, in the case of the book, the physical book costs somewhat more to produce, but the bulk of the production costs do not lie in the printing and binding of even the physical book, and the ebook is priced lower because the physical costs have been deducted.<br/><br/>In the case involving Aaron Swartz, what seems to have been overlooked in many of these comments is the law itself. In 1997, Congress passed the No Electronic Theft (NET) act. Those provisions, as codified in 17 USC § 506 (2)(2000) provide criminal punishment for reproducing or distributing, "including by electronic means, "during any 180-day period, of one or more copies or phonorecords of 1 or more copyrighted works having a value of more than $1,000". What is often overlooked or ignored is that while looking at or reading a copyrighted work electronically is not a violation, actually downloading a work is, under law, the reproduction of that work.<br/><br/>Like it or not, the physical facts in the case are not in dispute. Whether or not Aaron Swartz intended or did not intend to further distribute the more than four million articles he downloaded, it is absolutely clear that he electronically reproduced at least one copy of each of those articles and that those millions of articles had a sum total value of more than $1,000. He also did this under "guest" privileges used fraudulently, because, at the time, he was neither a student, nor did he register honestly when using the MIT system. Under the U.S. Code, that is against the law.<br/><br/>Whether the law is "right" or not is a totally separate question. If one wishes to live in a nation of laws, then there is the obligation to live by those laws, although, at least in the United States, there is certainly the possibility of changing that law. If one violates those laws, however, there is always the risk of being prosecuted. Aaron Swartz did not believe in the laws, as written for information, and violated them. He was prosecuted.<br/><br/>Should he have been prosecuted in the way the government did? How could the government ignore the illegal copying and downloading of more than four million files once it was brought to the attention of the Justice Department? Not to prosecute would have been an invitation to others to indulge wide-spread downloading and hacking/copying. Just as clearly, no one at MIT understood the significance of the case when they reported it.<br/><br/>Nor do the legal scholars who write articles and briefs attempting to distinguish between "minimal" incremental harm created by personal copying and larger harm created by "commercial" pirating. Admittedly, any individual's personal copying of electronic articles or books represents minimal economic harm to the copyright holder, but when thousands of individuals, or tens of thousands, download torrent e-book editions, or journal articles, or anything in mass, that individual minimal harm becomes a significant collective harm to the copyright holders… and as I've noted repeatedly, that harm is reflected already in sales figures.<br/><br/>But… the Justice Department cannot very well go after tens of thousands of individuals… if it could even locate them. All government prosecutors can do is to go after large instances of illegal copying and downloading… and Aaron Swartz was one of the largest.<br/><br/>In this case, if there is any party responsible for Swartz's suicide beside Swartz himself, it is not the federal government [and I'm no fan of the Justice Department], but MIT. MIT already had a trial program for open reading access to most of the files, one that, ironically, MIT made permanent after Swartz's death. If they wanted to punish Swartz, they could have revoked Swartz's access and any other privileges. They could have filed a civil suit against him or sought damages. From what I can tell, they did none of those. Instead, acting as so many university bureaucrats do, they passed the buck to the Justice Department. Then… when they realized what might happen, they begged and pleaded that DOJ not prosecute. What would have happened if DOJ had dropped all charges or only given Swartz a slap on the wrist? I can just see the headlines – "DOJ Caves to MIT" or "Feds Ignore Reddit Exec's Piracy." Obviously, DOJ could see them as well.<br/><br/>There are stories that suggest Swartz had attempted to get a plea bargain, but that DOJ refused any plea bargain that didn't involve jail time for Swartz, and that Swartz didn't believe he should serve time for something he didn't see as wrong. What Swartz – and some commenters here – didn't understand was that the law is the law, and that following personal feelings of right and wrong which conflict with the law often has a very high price… as it should, because, otherwise, everyone's personal feelings would be above the law… and that is a recipe for anarchy.<br/><br/>It's one thing to use those feelings – and those of others who share those views – to change the laws, and that can be constructive, but to flaunt the law and believe that there should be no significant consequences…?<br/><br/>In the end, however, given the outcry over cases such as that of Swartz, and the political pressures, those who want intellectual property cheaply, or for free, will likely win out in any venue where the product can be duplicated almost effortlessly and cheaply. The result will be that "popular" culture will sink even below mediocrity and that the vast majority of work of originality and quality will either be drowned out or lost in the flood of cheap presentations, or funded by wealthy patrons, as music and novels were in the late eighteenth century, or offered in some involved high-technology way that cannot be easily copied, thus making billions for a handful of media empires. Research will dwindle and be limited to the corporate sector in those areas where corporate security and "trade secrets" can offer some modicum of protection. All this cannot help but have a negative impact, both in terms of national productivity and creativity, because "free" does not pay for either productivity or creativity — or the support services they require — and very few people or organizations can afford to produce or create for free.<br/><br/>—L. E. Modesitt, Jr.: January 17th, 2013<br/><br/>36 Responses to "Law and "Right and Wrong""<br/><br/>And I don't know of ANY organization that can produce/create for free: even the ones who purportedly do things 'for free' do it with other people's money. What goes out from there is supposed to enrich society… or it gives them some manner of secondary gain (e.g. free cell phones when cell phone services and long term contracts are the real $$ makers).<br/><br/>'For free' almost always means advertising supported. And profits per view from advertising are so low that only media aimed at a truly massive number of people can even break even. The need to reach such a massive number of people means that creators always have to aim at the lowest common denominator just to stay in business. And there's the dystopian future of Mr. Modesitt's last paragraph.<br/><br/>The only entertainment business that stands a chance without the lowest common denominator approach is always-online video games, which are piracy resistant if not completely impervious. And, to say the least, video games have yet to display much artistic potential or even much desire to aim above the lowest common denominator.<br/><br/>Slight tangent here, J, but have to disagree. Many video games do only cater to the lowest common denominator. And many more rise above in the genre's they dwell in. For every Call of Duty X or Madden New Edition dealing in lowest we generally will have something like the Early Silent Hills or various individual efforts from Square that cross the line into true art and expression. I can honestly say I've never been frightened by a film or book the way Silent Hill 2 was able to mess with me. And while I would never hold them up to Mr. Modesitt's work or Robert Jordan's, I can honestly say that I have been every bit as moved by Terra's struggles in Final Fantasy VI, or Fei's search to find out what he really is in Xenogears.<br/><br/>The challenge there is less only appealing to the lowest common point and more to getting people to recognize and accept that these are art forms every bit as valid as literature, music and film.<br/><br/>Sorry for the derail. I honestly agree with everything Mr. Modesitt said, so can't really comment further unless someone else opens the arguement.<br/><br/>Copyright was invented to cope with a technical innovation: the printing press, in particular the depredations of publishers on authors. It would not be surprising if another technical innovation (computers) required it to change. Nevertheless, valuable work was produced before the invention of copyright. William Shakespeare, Homer both produced works of far greater value than either of us have, even with the benefit of copyright.<br/><br/>Before copyright, work was funded. Some by rich people, some by everyone. Now we have sites like indiegogo and kickstarter which have funded many creative works. 10% of the movies at the Sundance festival were funded by kickstarter, as well as one Oscar nominated movie. In the past the Church funded most work (all those illuminated manuscripts created during Europe's medieval era? Bach's Cantatas?). Rich patrons supported the arts (Classical music was not "popular"). The scientific work Aaron was interested in were not funded by the papers he downloaded. Nor was Newton's work funded by copyright, and it is original. It seems ludicrous to suggest that high quality creative work will stop if copyright ends. Just turn on your TV and tell me that the 500 channels of copyrighted crap encourage creative work.<br/><br/>Anarchy without law? Yes, to some extent. But never forget laws are bought and paid for by supporting pet politicians. When laws are only applied to the weak they lose their moral authority, and end up ignored. If Americans had respected British law, the US would still be a colony. I note that none of those responsible for the greatest recession since the great depression have been prosecuted. Just in the last month HSBC got away with drug money laundering, because they are too big to fail. Even you must admit that copyright violation pales in comparison to what the world's financial class has gotten away with. Law is a tool and historically only lasts as long as it is perceived as useful by the majority.<br/><br/>Now some technical points.<br/><br/>Technically there is no difference between downloading and viewing a document on your computer (read up on swap file, and browser caches if you care). JSTOR recognizes that fact in its terms of service. The fact whatever idiot lawyer you are quoting doesn't just shows his technical incompetence.<br/><br/>While you are good at pointing out others' hypocrisy, here is yours: this website runs on Linux, which was developed by many people in their spare time. Your argument would suggest Microsoft's products are better since they are funded by copyright. Yet Linux runs most websites. Over 60% of websites run the open source nginx and apache webservices. Every single copyrighted choice is technically inferior. So much for your argument. I notice you are even using Aaron's work on your website… Did you pay him for it? <br/><br/>The intellectual and real honesty of the people who developed the software that runs the internet is that most of the software you use to communicate on the internet is open source. The proprietary vendors have died. The most powerful change to our lives in the last 10 years has been made possible by people working in their spare time. Aaron was one of them. You may claim dishonesty but you do not clearly do not understand how the technology you take for granted came about. Windows, MacOS, the iPhone all contain open source software that make it possible for your computer to connect to and browse the internet. Had proprietary vendors had their way, you would still be stuck in AOL's walled garden.<br/><br/>Ultimately you are right on one score. You will lose if you only shout the same point again and again more and more shrilly. Perhaps copyright will be adapted to better suit the times, or perhaps it will break under the strain of new innovations. I hope we get a better system after the breakage, but that will depend very much on whether people like us come up with better ideas or cling to the way it's always been done.<br/><br/>There is one major difference between viewing and downloading. Viewing is cached and temporary. Downloading is the intent to make permanent, a distinction recognized by a number of court decisions. You're the one hanging your hat on technicalities here.<br/><br/>As for me, I've never claimed that copyright makes anything better. I've claimed that it grants rights and privileges and often income to the creator and tries to protect his or her rights. Once more, you're conflating issues.<br/><br/>It's not hypocritical to use open source material or documents, especially since those who created open source codes and systems did choose to offer them openly. It is hypocritical to claim honesty and then infringe copyright.<br/><br/>So… Swartz was both violating copyright, for which he was not charged, and for wire fraud and illegal use of the system, with which he was charged. That makes it better?<br/><br/>As for the banks, you might do me the honor of noting that I've been making, for years, the same point you have about banks and the financial community. I've repeatedly raised a wide range of issues about their actions and their lack of ethics, not to mention their ineptitude.<br/><br/>The documents were PDFs, PS, and Djvu. Browsers do not support inline reading of some or all of these formats. Therefore one must download the documents to read them. If I intend to delete them afterwards, it's not downloading??? I'm not convinced but then lawyers do tend to make the simplest things convoluted.<br/><br/>No it is not hypocritical to use open systems, but it is consistent for those who wanted them open to make them open and to advocate more openness. Open information advocates can be honest. Honest does not mean law-abiding. Gandhi broke the salt-laws in India because of his honest belief that they were wrong. He was honest, and a law breaker.<br/><br/>Fundamentally you seem to be arguing that you have rights over others because the law grants them to you, whereas it seems to me that it is because we all consent to follow the law that you effectively have those rights. We are granting you those rigths because we find it benefits us all. If we all decided to behave in some other way, those "rights" disappear because they never actually existed, unlike gravity which exists whatever I do. George Bush was correct when he said the Constitution is just some damn piece of paper — it is unless we follow it. As such there is value in conversing with those that want to change the system rather than railing against them.<br/><br/>Yes it does make it better if your statement is simply "I, LEM, am of the opinion that Swartz was violating copyright". I am not aware that this opinion has been established as a fact, but you are perfectly entitled to have an opinion, although I wish you said it was your opinion and not a fact.<br/><br/>I'm glad we agree on banks. Hopefully one day we will agree on copyright.<br/><br/>I've never disagreed with the idea that rights come from those with the power to establish and maintain them. In the USA, that power is represented, rightly or wrongly, by the three branches of government…and they established copyright. I have rights under copyright, but they aren't rights "over" anyone, not unless you're willing to say that everyone has rights over lawbreakers.<br/><br/>I have said, under the laws of Congress and the decisions of the courts, that the acts Swartz committed are a breach of copyright. Whether they should be is another question. You're arguing "should" as opposed to law. You have that right, and you even have the right to claim the law is wrong. But when you imply that Swartz and others have the right, because they believe it is right, to take actions,which if followed by others, cause me harm and which are proscribed by law, then you are advocating law-breaking. Under those circumstances, I think that taking me to task for pointing out that law-breaking is law-breaking is a bit much.<br/><br/>I am not arguing should. I am arguing fact. You claim copyright violation yet you present no evidence.<br/><br/>It may seem obvious that Swartz violated copyright, but it isn't. To claim copyright you cannot just have digitized papers you found in the public domain, which is mostly what JSTOR consists of. As you must know from having published books, there must be a clear trail of paperwork from the author to the current copyright owner. Along this trail someone must have registered the copyright. It is a felony to forge this paperwork, just like it was a felony to "robo-sign" mortgage transfers.<br/><br/>If you do have evidence that Swartz violated copyright, please present it. Otherwise you making a claim based on an assumption. I.e. you have an opinion.<br/><br/>While I do not have evidence that there was no copyright violation, I find it unlikely that Swartz violated copyright because the prosecutor found 4 things to charge Aaron with, and then found another 9. Copyright violation would be a no-brainer, yet for someone with such zeal, s/he did not charge him with copyright violation. My guess is it really wouldn't stick. This is surprising since the wire-fraud claims were technically very weak as was pointed out in a link I posted previously.<br/><br/>I read your previous post as stating violating copyright is intellectually dishonest. I disagree with that statement. I do agree that it is breaking the law.<br/><br/>As for proof in the legal sense… you know as well as I do that only a court could judge that. I do have evidence. Four million articles are a hell of a lot. Tell me. Tell me that every single article was in the public domain. Turning the argument around… you can't.<br/><br/>And if it was so legal, why did the most honorable Mr. Swartz have to log on illegally [and there is proof of that] and break into a restricted space to park his laptop?<br/><br/>Come on, Joe. He broke the law. You're justifying all his actions on the basis that you believe the law is wrong. As I've said before, that may be. But it is the law.<br/><br/>LEM, you're not following basic logic. If you posit something you must provide evidence for it. If you do not posit something, you do not have to provide evidence for it not existing. If you state there is a spaghetti monster in the sky you have to provide evidence for it. I who make no such claim do not have to provide evidence for it not existing. You posit Swartz violated copyright. Where is your evidence? I do not claim that he did not violate copyright, but I don't claim that he did either, and I see reasonable grounds for believing it is questionable that he did which I stated above. You appear to believe without a doubt that he did. The burden of proof is on you, not on me.<br/><br/>He changed his MAC address. That is not uncommon practice. That is illegal and punishable in Massachusetts by $100 fine. He should have been fined. We can agree on that.<br/><br/>The freeloaders needed a martyr (Joe is already making comparisons to Gandhi), and now that they have one they will defend him by whatever means necessary until Google can get a street named after him.<br/><br/>The fact is he broke the law, Joe thinks this is fine because he doesn't like the law, he wants a revolution against the 'oppression' of copyright–our main legal means of allowing creators to choose whether or not they give their work away for free. It does not say JSTOR's collection includes only 500,000 documents from before 1923.<br/><br/>Nor did I say Aaron was like Gandhi. Simply make your proof…shaky.<br/><br/>For my two cents:<br/><br/>If you are utilizing some one elses network, storage facility, library, etc to obtain something, and they wish to charge you, that is their right as the holders of the item in question. But as the maintainer of that property (Intellectual, physical or some other form I'm missing out on!) they may determine the disposition of that property.<br/><br/>Simply because something is in public domain, say a book of Shakespeare's plays, does not mean you get that book for free. Be it electronic or physical media, I must pay for that book. Now, if I locate some one who will give me a copy of that media, for free, which is considered a part of the public domain… Well, that's a different ball of wax entirely. The holder of the media is offering it for free. It's public domain, there for I am violating no copyrights. I'm just fine in accepting the book of plays.<br/><br/>However, if the media is not in the public domain and you offered me a complete collection of LEM's works in digital format with out paying for the additional copy, which I then accepted, we would both be guilty of piracy and copyright infringement. The above provisos would still need to be addressed. My owning a digital, or physical copy already does not clear the way for carte blanche to do as I please in getting another copy.<br/><br/>You're right in the legal sense, and that's probably why DOJ went for wire fraud charges. It's also why Al Capone went to jail for income tax evasion, rather than murder or various other gang-related charges. HOWEVER, Swartz downloaded 4.8 million articles. There was no way he could even have known what was in at least 99% of them [one tenth of one percent amounting to 4,800]. To me and to any reasonable person, it would seem to me,that suggests that either he didn't care in the slightest whether he was breaking copyright or he fully intended to. He was trying to make a statement without fully considering the costs to himself, or for that matter, to anyone else. There is also the question of whether someone did something and whether he was found guilty. Under law, only the verdict counts, but that doesn't necessarily mean that he didn't do it… or break the law in doing it. As Joe and I have both pointed out, scores of finance executives have committed fraud and were never charged, let alone convicted, and there's plenty of evidence… but the resources required to make the case would be staggering, especially against the legal talent mustered.<br/><br/>There seems to be a disturbing trend in academic publishing as in order to be published, one must assign over the reproduction rights to the corporation which is doing the publishing, who will then profit (or not) off of reproducing the paper.<br/><br/>This artificially restricts the mostly non-corporately sponsored output of academia, and increases cost as each research institution needs to purchase access to the papers published.<br/>This decreases the already scant money available to universities for research purposes – not to be confused with the total budget of a US university, much of which seems to be dedicated to professional sports subsidies, monuments to the glory of board egos and management.<br/><br/>It is somewhat understandable if this leads idealistic individuals to conclude that the current system is broken, particularly with the propaganda of "all politicians are the same, change is impossible so make sure you keep what you have at any cost" as well as the lack of propaganda from collective action groups.<br/>Thus, they take action, assuming that society will be magically transformed by their actions, possibly by martyring themselves and causing a shift of popular (and after that, official) opinion in their favour. The consequences of their actions to themselves are – for most – assumed to be minimal.<br/>I must be clear that this is not meant to be a insult to the people who do act based on their ideals, but that most people who do so do not have a realistic opinion of how likely they are to be successful nor how likely they are to get hurt by them.<br/><br/>There have been idealists who have succeeded, even while martyring themselves intentionally, but almost all who have done so have done it with a support organisation (often created or supported by the individual themselves).<br/>The stories told about them are, however, often simplified and only pay attention to the last actions taken that magically cause the desired effect. For instance, how many people fully grok that Gandhi spent 21 years in South Africa rather than, as most documentaries about popular heroes do, treat it as a brief work/vacation visit?<br/>"Gandhi went to South Africa, practised as a lawyer and discovered that racism was Bad. He then went back to India, dressed in a loincloth and freed the country from the now-oppressive (but previously remarkably civilized) British Empire. And they all lived happily ever after. The End."<br/><br/>Another interesting discussion might be about how social media, allowing for extensive self-selection of peers and like-minded individuals, cause individuals to over-estimate how common their opinions are – and thus erroneously believe that the time for standing up to the Man has come.<br/>I doubt that it is just the elites that find their version of the Truths to be Self-Evident these days.<br/><br/>"There is no justice in following unjust laws. It's time to come into the light and, in the grand tradition of civil disobedience, declare our opposition to this private theft of public culture.–Aaron Swartz, "Guerilla Open Access Manifesto", 2008<br/><br/>After this 'Manifesto' was 'published' Aaron Swartz wrote a program in 2008 to legally download and post federal court documents for free online using free access via public libraries, rather than the few cents per page that the government charges through its electronic archive, PACER. The FBI declined to press charges.<br/><br/>For the present case, prosecutors accused Swartz of hacking into MIT's system in November 2010 after breaking into a computer wiring closet on campus. Prosecutors said he intended to distribute the articles on file-sharing websites.<br/><br/>The debate here and the previous Blog entry has primarily focused upon the issue of distributing 'the articles on file-sharing websites', copyright law etc. The issue of physically 'breaking into a computer wiring closet on campus' has been largely ignored.<br/><br/>Aaron Swartz played a long-term cat-and-mouse game with MIT and JSTOR. They repeatedly tried to get him off the network and he repeatedly used different pseudonyms to get back on. He broke into the 'computer wiring closet' because he was trying to find a way to do what MIT and JSTOR were trying to block. He knew that he was doing something illegal and he was trying not to get caught; he hid his face from the video camera in the MIT closet. When the police spotted him, he wasn't surprised that they wanted to talk to him; he jumped off his bike and tried to outrun the police on foot.<br/><br/>Aaron Swartz stated the end (of publicly sharing information) justified the means (of breaking existing laws including breaking & entering with the intent to commit a felony) to obtain it. He proved he would follow his words with actions in 2008 and so too in 2010. Was he arrogant or naive to believe he may get away with the MIT job, too? Was he arrogant or naive to believe that he wouldn't suffer any consequences on the chance he did get caught?<br/><br/>The great tradition of civil disobedience is to intentionally violate the law and proudly bear the consequences in order to change public opinion and eventually change the law, not to violate the law in secret and try to render the law you oppose unenforceable while avoiding punishment. With his trial about to begin and given the chance to uphold the tradition of civil disobedience by taking advantage of the opportunity to publicize his desire to change existing laws in a meaningful way Aaron Swartz…..does what? Commit suicide. Did he martyr himself or was he a coward? Did his depression problems leave him defenseless and helpless to take advantage of the opportunity he made for himself? (Before the self righteous indignantly accuse me of insensitivity toward the very real problem of depression, you would be accusing one who had it and overcame it. Got it? Good!).<br/><br/>Did prosecutors drive him to suicide? His family and supporters need someone to blame for their own comfort and the prosecutors are easy targets. I doubt they were<br/>harder on him than they are on other defendant. Given the "Guerilla Open Access Manifesto", Aaron Swartz started down this road at his own initiative. Did he fail to understand that he was now entering the playground of adults and that game is played for keeps? Or didn't he have what it took to follow through on the tradition of civil disobedience and on his vision and accept the consequences, good or bad, for it?<br/><br/>One thing is certain: Aaron Swartz started a fight that he was not up to the task to finish himself…<br/><br/>Brief belated notes from my computer industry experience and conversations with my dad (columbia copyright law professor): first, characterization of open source is incorrect. It doesn't dominate markets; my rule of thumb is that open source acceptance means a commodity and competition has shifted elsewhere. Linux does not dominate the market. It is not a better product than Windows — its parent, Unix, has fundamental flaws in access control and I/O, which make its security lag and its standardization not complete (database companies bypass the I/O) — I was around at the time Unix was developed. The electronic frontier foundation was founded and endures because companies and the government simply were using the law to protect monopoly, not just to protect the interests of authors or the companies themselves.<br/><br/>With regard to copyright (and patent), copyright was intended to protect individual rights against other individuals, but it has historically done a poor job of protecting individual authors/inventors against companies, which is the reason why copyright length kept getting extended (authors' families needed the income). Motown's Gordy Berry was notorious for ensuring that no author wound up with anything meaningful for his or her contributions. The creation of a global market for celebrity has resulted in a much more winner-take-all kind of market, which has allowed more authors to prosper but has mostly empowered the companies that can sell to this market. Any solution that trusts the companies to pass back enforced copyright revenues (or patent revenues) to an individual author where appropriate is far from optimal.<br/><br/>It sounds to me that Joe has a basic misunderstanding of ownership. He also has a problem with personal ownership of property. Joe are you a socialist? One for all and all for one. Nothing for me. But everything for you? Wake up man this is a capitalist country. People want to make money on things they create. Its called theft when you take it without permission, whether it is real property or intellectual property. The new economy is a joke, people just haven't figured out how to make money on it yet. The problem will be solved and people like you can go back to your socialized fantasy worlds.<br/><br/>Jack, I doubt that joe is a socialist whatever that means; the word gets thrown around as a slur quite a bit.<br/><br/>What joe seems to be is conflating the discussion of what is – current law and how Schwartz most likely violated it – and what ought to be (according to joe) – as in, information should be more free. Unfortunately for us all, there doesn't seem to be a possibility for a discussion as joe has left and seemed to be using his own views as unassailable truths from what I could tell.<br/><br/>That doesn't mean that we can't have a spirited discussion about copyright/intellectual property versus physical property, theft and so on, which is why I will respond to your post myself.<br/><br/>There is a bit of a difference between physical property and intellectual property (IP), and that is the problem that with IP, even if I steal it from you, you (usually) still have your IP. Most people consider using an Xerox machine to copy a book a lesser crime than outright nabbing the book and running away.<br/><br/>Now, with electronic intellectual property, copying becomes very easy to do as well as less of a burden on the one you copy from (I remember Xeroxing pages out of a book in grade school – it seemed like it took forever!).<br/><br/>Your optimistic viewpoint about people solving the problem of unauthorized copying is somewhat unfounded. People have been making money on intellectual property for quite some time (granted, now as then it is seldom the creator that gains the most profit), and we have had problems with pirated books (even pirate printers) since way back when.<br/><br/>Unfortunately (for intellectual property purposes), electronic intellectual property doesn't help out much either. Almost all computers these days are general purpose computing machines, and what that means is that there is no one with a master key, no one with full control over the computer except the person with physical access.<br/>This means that if and when you wish to sell intellectual property to someone and the target platform is a computer, you can not be certain that the computer you've given the ability to access your content won't be sharing that content on the side (or just storing it for its own retrieval later).<br/>The reason for this is that even if you're making your own programs for a computer, the people who run the program on their machines can program the machine to lie, and can dissect and change the program. There is a very active and competent association of people doing so with new games (often breaking the copy-protection within hours of releases).<br/><br/>There have been several initiatives to lock down PCs; none of them have succeeded as of yet. There are advantages to have a non-locked-down computer – just as there are advantages to having a locked-down computer as well.<br/><br/>The only economically viable solution that has been found to this issue is to keep the intellectual property elsewhere and allow the local computer only partial access to it. Even so, this only works for dynamic content, as even you'd only let me see pictures of each page of – for instance – a book streamed across the 'net, I could still save each image. The same goes for audio – even if you make it hard for me to record the sound in the computer, I can always record it at the output instead.<br/><br/>If you are interested in learning more why it is not an easy problem to solve, I would be happy to lay out more details, but I did not want people to have the opinion that this is something that is going to be easy to solve.<br/><br/>The thought that Aaron Swartz may have been 'helped' in his suicide occurred to me, too. However, his most ardent supporters have not made this charge….yet….at least in the sources I've consulted. Perhaps I'm not reading the right sources.<br/><br/>One thing is clear: Aaron Swartz knew his method of obtaining those files and what he planned to do with them was illegal under existing laws. That seems to be lost on some who are arguing from 'what ought to be' rather than arguing to 'what is'.<br/><br/>It could be that Joe knows something. I have read the harpers.com blog:<br/><br/>"In the Swartz case, the prosecutors claimed they were acting on behalf of two injured parties — JSTOR and MIT. But JSTOR disagreed with this characterization, including the attorneys' use of the word "theft," and demanded that they drop the case"<br/><br/>"Nancy Gertner, a recently retired federal judge who is intimately familiar with both prosecutors, lambasted them in a broadcast interview, parsing and ridiculing the claims they had made against Swartz and suggesting that the case should have been dismissed"<br/><br/>"The details that have emerged since Swartz's death have only strengthened calls for the removal and punishment of the prosecutors. Swartz's lawyers revealed, for example, that when their client's suicidal nature was noted during their failed efforts to get the charges reduced to a misdemeanor level, Heymann responded by saying "Fine, we'll lock him up." Prosecutors were also revealed to have offered a reduced sentence, but only if Swartz pleaded guilty to every charge. This is clear evidence of oppression geared to advance prosecutorial careers, not to serve the interests of justice. Britain's Daily Mail showed that Swartz was not the only youthful alleged hacker whom Heymann had hounded to suicide — twenty-four-year-old Jonathan James took his own life in 2008, six months after his home was searched in a raid coordinated by Heymann. The DOJ undertook no internal probe of that case, instead giving Heymann an award for "distinguished service.""<br/><br/>"The flaw in Ortiz's posture has been laid bare by Chief Judge Alex Kozinski of the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals. In United States v. Nosal, he dismissed the theory Ortiz used to go after Swartz, saying it would potentially criminalize "everyone who uses a computer in violation of computer use restrictions — which may well include everyone who uses a computer." Kozinski was born and raised in Communist Romania, and knows a thing or two about totalitarian states — and he knows that prosecutorial overbreadth is their leitmotif."<br/><br/>Just finished battalion, good stuff as usual. I really do enjoy your Nameless ideas and concepts, as transposed into today's society. | Dlí agus "ceart agus mícheart"<br/><br/>Ní léiríonn aon trácht ar mo phost deireanach ach conas is féidir le dearcthaí pearsanta tuairimí a dath de chúrsaí, go háirithe dlí. Mar sin, déanaimis labhairt faoin dlí. Go ginearálta, is é an dlí coiriúil códú na gcionta sin a bhfuil an tsochaí tar éis a théarma a dhí-ghlactha a bheith déanta agus a bhfuil an t-údarás rialaithe tar éis an cion nó an raon pionós a chódú agus a fhógairt, agus an dlí sin a shainiú, agus pionós nó raon pionóis a ghearradh orthu, lena sáraítear an dlí sin. Ní "idéalach" é an dlí; is cód é. Ní aontaíonn an cód sin i gcónaí leis an méid saoránach a cheapann sé a bheith, nó fiú na forálacha a bhíonn i gceist leis. Sin cúis amháin le haghaidh cúirteanna, agus cén fáth go bhfuil sé de chumhacht ag Cúirt Uachtarach na Stát Aontaithe athbhreithniú a dhéanamh ar dhlíthe arna rith ag an gComhdháil, nó ag rialtais stáit agus áitiúla.<br/><br/>Ar dtús, bhí sárú cóipchirt ina ábhar dlí sibhialta go docht, ach d’éirigh sáruithe cóipchirt chomh mór sin i ndeireadh na naoú haois déag gur achtaigh an Chomhdháil na chéad pionóis choiriúla don sárú cóipchirt, ach i níos lú ná deich mbliana bhí gá le cur chuige níos cuimsithí, agus d’éirigh an chéad dlí leathan le sárú cóipchirt a dhéanamh i 1909. Ó shin i leith, cuireadh forálacha éagsúla leis an dlí um chóipcheart coiriúil, lena n-áirítear an tAcht um Chloígh Leictreonach (NET) i 1997, agus leis an Acht um Chóipcheart Lár Tíre Digiteach de 1998.<br/><br/>Mar a thugtar faoi deara ag an gcuid is mó de na scoláirí maithe dlí maoine, níl an mhaoin teoranta go dlíthiúil do rudaí inláimhsithe, ach tá cuid mhór de na conspóide maidir le sárú cóipchirt nó goid maoine intleachtúla ar an bhfíric nach gcuireann an chuid is mó de na daoine an luach mhothúchánach céanna ar chóipeáil an ríomhleabhar nó íoslódáil ceoil agus bheadh siad ar dhuine ag glacadh cóip fhisiciúil den leabhar céanna nó CD ceoil ó gan íoc as. Ach cuireann an dlí reatha an luach céanna ar ghoid leabhar digiteach nó cóip dhigiteach údaraithe d'amhrán a bhfuil leabhar fisiceach nó CD ceoil air. Agus ba chóir, mar gheall ar an siopa leabhar, an mórdhíoltóir, agus an t-údar, go léiríonn cóip saor in aisce "cúna" caillteanas an-dáiríre.Is díol dearmadta é, díreach mar a léiríonn goid leabhar fisiceach nó CD díol amháin. Ina theannta sin, i gcás an leabhair, cosnaíonn an leabhar fisiceach beagán níos mó a tháirgeadh, ach ní luíonn formhór na gcostas táirgthe i priontáil agus i gceangal an leabhair choirp fiú, agus tá an ríomhleabhar ar phraghas níos ísle toisc go bhfuil na costais fhisiciúla asbhainte.<br/><br/>Sa chás ina bhfuil Aaron Stewart, a bhfuil an chuma air go ndearnadh dearmad air i gcuid mhaith de na tuairimí seo féin. Sa bhliain 1997, rith an Chomhdháil an gníomh Gan Trácht Leictreonach (NET). Soláthraíonn na forálacha sin, arna n-aithint i 17 MSU § 506 (2)(2000) pionós coiriúil chun cóipeanna amháin nó níos mó d’oibreacha faoi chóipcheart a atáirgeadh nó a dháileadh, "lena n-áirítear trí mheán leictreonach, le linn aon tréimhse 180 lá, de chóipeanna nó de phorecairds de bhreis ar $ 1,000". Is é an rud a ndéantar dearmad air nó a neamhaird ná, cé nach sárú é obair faoi chóipcheart a léamh nó a léamh go leictreonach, saothar a íoslódáil i ndáiríre, faoin dlí, atáirgeadh na hoibre sin.<br/><br/>Cosúil leis nó nach bhfuil, níl na fíricí fisiciúla sa chás faoi dhíospóid. Cibé acu an raibh nó nach raibh Aaron Stewart beartaithe nó nach raibh sé ar intinn aige a thuilleadh a dháileadh níos mó ná ceithre mhilliún alt a íoslódáil sé, tá sé fíor-soiléir go bhfuil sé a atáirgeadh go leictreonach ar a laghad cóip amháin de gach ceann de na hailt sin agus go raibh suim níos mó ná $ 1,000 ag na milliúin earraí. Rinne sé é seo faoi phribhléidí "aoi" a úsáideadh go calaoiseach, mar gheall ar, ag an am, ní raibh sé ina mhac léinn, ná ní raibh sé ag clárú go hionraic nuair a bhí an córas MIT á úsáid aige.Faoi Chód SAM, is é sin i gcoinne an dlí.<br/><br/>Cibé an bhfuil an dlí "ceart" nó nach bhfuil ceist iomlán ar leith. Má sháraíonn duine na dlíthe sin, áfach, tá an baol ann go ndéanfaí ionchúiseamh orthu i gcónaí. Ina theannta sin, is ionann cóipeáil phearsanta aon duine d'earraí nó leabhair leictreonacha agus dochar eacnamaíoch íosta don sealbhóir cóipchirt, ach nuair a dhéanann na mílte duine, nó na mílte duine, eagrán r-leabhair nó alt irisí torrent a íoslódáil, nó aon rud i mais, go ndéantar dochar suntasach comhchoiteann don sealbhóir cóipchirt... agus mar a thug mé faoi deara arís agus arís eile, déantar an dochar sin i bhfigiúirí díolacháin cheana féin.<br/><br/>Ach... ní féidir leis an Roinn Dlí agus Cirt dul go han-mhaith tar éis na mílte daoine... dá bhféadfadh sé fiú iad a aimsiú. E. Modesitt, Jr.: Eanáir 17th, 2013<br/><br/>36 Freagraíonn an Dlí agus "An Ceart agus an Wrong"<br/><br/>Agus níl a fhios agam ar aon eagraíocht ar féidir léi/a chruthú saor in aisce: fiú na cinn a d’fhéadfadh rudaí a dhéanamh 'saor in aisce' é a dhéanamh le hairgead daoine eile. Cad a théann amach as tá ceaptha sochaí a shaibhriú... nó tugann sé bealach éigin dóibh gnóthachan tánaisteach (m.sh. fóin phóca saor in aisce nuair a bhíonn seirbhísí teileafóin phóca agus conarthaí fadtéarmacha fíor $ déantóir).<br/><br/>Le haghaidh saor in aisce 'mórthaíocht' ciallaíonn beagnach i gcónaí fógraíocht tacaíocht., Agus tá brabúis in aghaidh an dearcadh ionas nach féidir ach na meáin atá dírithe ar líon fíor ollmhór daoine a bhriseadh fiú. Ciallaíonn an gá atá le teacht ar líon chomh ollmhór daoine go bhfuil creators i gcónaí chun aidhm ag an denominator coitianta is ísle ach fanacht i ngnó. Agus níl an todhchaí dystopian de tUasal Modhets deiridh mhír.<br/><br/>Is é an gnó siamsaíochta amháin a sheasann deis gan an cur chuige denominator coitianta is ísle i gcónaí cluichí físeáin ar líne, atá resistant píoráideacht más rud é nach bhfuil go hiomlán go dodhéanta. Agus, a rá leis an méid is lú, cluichí físeáin go bhfuil fós a thaispeáint i bhfad ar chumas ealaíne nó fiú fonn chun d'aidhm os cionn an denominator is ísle coitianta.<br/><br/>Líomhnáiste anseo, J, ach tá a easaontas., a lán cluichí físeáin a dhéanamh freastal ar ach amháin ar an denominator is ísle coitianta., Agus go leor eile a ardú thuas sa seánra tá siad dwell i. I gcás gach Call of Duty X nó Madden New Edition déileáil i is ísle beidh orainn rud éigin cosúil leis na luatha Ciúin Cnoic nó iarrachtaí aonair éagsúla ó Cearnóg a thrasnaíonn an líne isteach fíor-ealaín agus léiriú. Is féidir liom a rá go hionraic go raibh eagla orm riamh le scannán nó leabhar ar an mbealach a bhí Silicon Hill 2 in ann a praiseach a dhéanamh liom. Agus cé nach mbeadh mé iad a shealbhú suas go dtí obair an tUasal Modhets nó Robert Jordan's, is féidir liom a rá go hionraic go bhfuil mé gach giotán mar atá ar athraíodh a ionad ag streachailt Terra i final Fantasy VI, nó Fei cuardaigh a fháil amach cad a bhfuil sé i ndáiríre i Xenagears.<br/><br/>Níl an dúshlán ach níos lú achomharc a dhéanamh ar an bpointe comónta is ísle agus níos mó chun daoine a fháil chun aitheantas a thabhairt do na foirmeacha ealaíne seo agus glacadh leo ar gach rud chomh bailí le litríocht, ceol agus scannán.<br/><br/>Tá brón orm don dallamullóg a chur orm agus aontaím go hionraic le gach rud a dúirt an tUasal Modhannaot, mar sin ní féidir leis seo a thuilleadh ach amháin má osclaíonn duine eile an argóint.<br/><br/>Cuireadh tús le cóipcheart chun déileáil le nuálaíocht theicniúil: an preas priontála, go háirithe d’ísliú foilsitheoirí ar údair.Ní bheadh iontas air dá n-athródh nuálaíocht theicniúil (ríomhairí) eile. Mar sin féin, cuireadh obair luachmhar ar fáil roimh an aireagán cóipchirt, d'oibrigh William Shakespeare, an dá chuid a tháirgtear i bhfad níos mó luacha ná ceachtar de na againn, fiú amháin le leas an chóipchirt.<br/><br/>Roimh chóipcheart, maoiníodh cuid den obair ag daoine saibhre, cuid acu siúd a bhfuil suíomhanna cosúil le indigo agus kickster a bhfuil go leor saothar cruthaitheach maoinithe acu. I rith na tréimhse seo, bhí 10% de na scannáin i bhFéilte Sundance á maoiniú ag tús an t-am ar fad, agus bhí an chuid is mó den obair á maoiniú ag Oscar as an Eaglais (iad siúd a bhí ar an bhfód le linn ré mheánaoiseach na hEorpa? Ní raibh aon rath ar an saothar eolaíochta Aaron, a d’fhág na páipéir a d’íoslódáil sé. Ní raibh aon obair Newton á maoiniú ag cóipcheart, agus tá sé bunaidh. Dealraíonn sé go bhfuil obair chruthaitheach ar ardchaighdeán stopadh má thagann deireadh le cóipcheart. Cas ar do theilifís agus inis dom go spreagann na 500 cainéal de chruth cruthaitheach.<br/><br/>Anarchy gan dlí?Sea, go pointe áirithe. Ach ní dhéantar dearmad ar dhlíthe a cheannach agus a íoc trí thacú le polaiteoirí peataí. Nuair a chuirtear dlíthe i bhfeidhm ach ar an lag a chailleann siad a n-údarás morálta, agus deireadh suas neamhaird. Dá n-urramódh Meiriceánaigh dlí na Breataine, bheadh na Stáit Aontaithe fós ina gcoilíneacht. Tugaim faoi deara nach ndearnadh aon cheann de na daoine atá freagrach as an cúlú is mó ó rinneadh an dúlagar mór a ionchúiseamh. Díreach sa mhí dheireanach fuair HSBC amach le sciúradh airgid drugaí, toisc go bhfuil siad ró-mhór le teip.Fiú ní mór duit a admháil go bhfuil an dlí maidir le sárú cóipchirt i gcomparáid leis an méid a bhfuil an t-aicme airgeadais domhanda faighte amach leis. Is uirlis é an dlí agus maireann sé go stairiúil ach amháin chomh fada agus a mheasann an t-romlach é.<br/><br/>Anois, roinnt pointí teicniúla.<br/><br/>Go teicniúil níl aon difríocht idir doiciméad a íoslódáil agus a fheiceáil ar do ríomhaire (léigh suas ar chomhad babhtáil, agus taisce bhrabhsálaí má tá tú cúram). Aithníonn JSTOR go bhfuil a chuid téarmaí seirbhíse. Is cuma cén dlíodóir dhíoigh atá á lua agat ní thaispeánann sé ach a neamhinniúlacht theicniúil.<br/><br/>Cé go bhfuil tú go maith ag cur in iúl go bhfuil hypocrisy daoine eile, anseo tá mise: an suíomh gréasáin seo ritheann ar Linux, a d'fhorbair go leor daoine ina gcuid ama spártha.Mófá táirgí Microsoft ós rud é go bhfuil siad maoinithe ag cóipcheart. An chuid is mó de láithreáin ghréasáin eile.Tá níos mó ná 60% de na suíomhanna Gréasáin ag rith na seirbhísí gréasáin foinse oscailte agus ag gach rogha faoi chóipcheart.Tá an oiread sin do do argóint.Tugann mé faoi deara go bhfuil tú ag baint úsáide as saothar Aaron ar do láithreán gréasáin... An ndearna tú íoc air as?<br/><br/>Is é an macántacht intleachtúil agus fíor-mhonarlach na ndaoine a d’fhorbair na bogearraí a ritheann an t-idirlíon ná go bhfuil an chuid is mó de na bogearraí a úsáideann tú chun cumarsáid a dhéanamh ar an idirlíon oscailte.Tá na díoltóirí dílseánaigh tar éis bás a fháil. Is é an t-athrú is cumhachtaí dár saol le 10 mbliana anuas a d'fhéadfadh daoine atá ag obair ina gcuid ama spártha. Is féidir leat éileamh mímhacántacht ach ní gá duit a thuiscint go soiléir conas a ghlac tú an teicneolaíocht a tugadh faoi Windows, MacOS, an iPhone Tá gach bogearraí foinse oscailte a fhágann gur féidir do ríomhaire ceangal leis agus brabhsáil ar an idirlíon. Bhí a mbealach féin ag díoltóirí dílseánaigh, bheadh tú fós i bhfostú i ngairdín Theallach AOL.<br/><br/>Ar deireadh thiar tá tú ceart ar scór amháin.Caillfidh tú mura ndéanann tú an pointe céanna a dhúnadh arís agus níos mó arís agus níos mó go gairid.B'fhéidir go ndéanfar cóipcheart a oiriúnú chun freastal ar na huaire, nó b'fhéidir go mbrisfidh sé faoi bhrú nuálaíochtaí nua. Tá súil agam go bhfaighimid córas níos fearr tar éis an tsro briseadh, ach braithfidh sé sin go mór ar cibé an dtagann daoine orainn le smaointe níos fearr nó an gcloistear ar an mbealach a dhéantar i gcónaí é.<br/><br/>Is é an rud is tábhachtaí ná roinnt cinntí cúirte a dhéanamh, de réir sainmhínithe, idirdhealú a dhéanamh, agus roinnt cinntí cúirte, agus do hata a chrochadh ar theicníochtaí anseo.<br/><br/>Maidir liom féin, níor mhothaigh mé riamh go ndéanann cóipcheart aon rud níos fearr. Tá réimse leathan saincheisteanna ardaithe agam arís agus arís eile faoina ngníomhartha agus a easpa eitice, gan trácht ar a n-neamhoiriúnacht.<br/><br/>Ní thacaíonn na brabhsálaithe le léamh inlíne ar roinnt de na formáidí seo nó ar fad.Dá bhrí sin, ní mór ceann amháin de na doiciméid a íoslódáil chun iad a léamh.Má tá sé ar intinn agam iad a scriosadh ina dhiaidh sin, ní íoslódáil??? Níl mé cinnte ach ansin bíonn claonadh ag dlíodóirí na rudaí is simplí a dhéanamh i gcomhthráth.<br/><br/>Níl sé hipitéiseach córais oscailte a úsáid, ach tá sé comhsheasmhach dóibh siúd a bhí ag iarraidh iad a oscailt agus a spreagadh níos oscailteacht.Is féidir abhcóidí eolais oscailte a bheith macánta. Ní chiallaíonn Macánta dlí-géilliúil. Chuir Gandhi isteach na dlíthe salainn san India mar gheall ar a chreideamh macánta go raibh siad mícheart. Bhí sé macánta, agus briseadh dlí.<br/><br/>Go bunúsach is cosúil go bhfuil tú ag argóint go bhfuil cearta agat thar daoine eile mar go dtugann an dlí iad a thabhairt duit, ach is cosúil go bhfuil sé domsa go bhfuil sé toisc go dtoil againn ar fad an dlí go bhfuil na cearta sin agat go héifeachtach a leanúint. Tá na cearta sin á ndeonú againn toisc go mbaineann sé go mór linn go léir. Má shocraigh muid ar fad iad féin a iompar ar bhealach éigin eile, imíonn na "cearta" sin toisc nach raibh siad ann i ndáiríre riamh, murab ionann agus domhan a bhfuil ann is cuma cad a dhéanfaidh mé. Bhí George Bush ceart nuair a dúirt sé nach bhfuil sa Bhunreacht ach píosa diabhal páipéir – mura leanaimid air. Níl a fhios agam gur bunaíodh an tuairim seo mar fhíoras, ach tá tú i dteideal tuairim a fháil, cé gur mian liom go mbeadh sé mar thuairim agat agus ní ar fíric é.<br/><br/>Tá áthas orm go n-aontaíonn muid ar bhainc. Tá an ceart sin agat, agus tá sé de cheart agat fiú an dlí a éileamh mícheart. Ós rud é go gcaithfidh eolas a bheith agat ar leabhair a fhoilsiú, ní mór rian soiléir páipéir a bheith ann ón údar go dtí an t-úinéir cóipchirt reatha.
